sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।षट्त्रिंशे त्वथ संप्राप्ते वर्षे कौरवनन्दनः ।ददर्श विपरीतानि निमित्तानि युधिष्ठिरः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ṣaṭtriṁśe tvatha saṁprāpte varṣe kauravanandanaḥ ,dadarśa viparītāni nimittāni yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said: Then, when the thirty-sixth year had arrived, Yudhishthira, the delight of the Kurus, saw adverse omens.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: Then, when the thirty-sixth year had arrived, Yudhishthira, the delight of the Kurus, saw adverse omens.

sanskrit: ववुर्वाताः सनिर्घाता रूक्षाः शर्करवर्षिणः ।अपसव्यानि शकुना मण्डलानि प्रचक्रिरे ॥२॥
transliteration: vavurvātāḥ sanirghātā rūkṣāḥ śarkaravarṣiṇaḥ ,apasavyāni śakunā maṇḍalāni pracakrire.
english: Winds blew, accompanied by thunder, harsh and showering gravel. Birds formed ominous, counter-clockwise circles.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. Winds blew, accompanied by thunder, harsh and showering gravel. Birds formed ominous, counter-clockwise circles.

sanskrit: प्रत्यगूहुर्महानद्यो दिशो नीहारसंवृताः ।उल्काश्चाङ्गारवर्षिण्यः प्रपेतुर्गगनाद्भुवि ॥३॥
transliteration: pratyagūhurmahānadyo diśo nīhārasaṁvṛtāḥ ,ulkāścāṅgāravarṣiṇyaḥ prapeturgaganādbhuvi.
english: Great rivers flowed backwards, the directions were enveloped in mist, and meteors showering embers fell from the sky to the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. Great rivers flowed backwards, the directions were enveloped in mist, and meteors showering embers fell from the sky to the earth.

sanskrit: आदित्यो रजसा राजन्समवच्छन्नमण्डलः ।विरश्मिरुदये नित्यं कबन्धैः समदृश्यत ॥४॥
transliteration: ādityo rajasā rājansamavacchannamaṇḍalaḥ ,viraśmirudaye nityaṁ kabandhaiḥ samadṛśyata.
english: O king, the sun, its disk completely obscured by dust, regularly appeared rayless at sunrise, resembling (or surrounded by) ominous headless forms (kabandha).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. O king, the sun, its disk completely obscured by dust, regularly appeared rayless at sunrise, resembling (or surrounded by) ominous headless forms (kabandha).

sanskrit: परिवेषाश्च दृश्यन्ते दारुणाः चन्द्रसूर्ययोः ।त्रिवर्णाः श्यामरूक्षान्तास्तथा भस्मारुणप्रभाः ॥५॥
transliteration: pariveṣāśca dṛśyante dāruṇāḥ candrasūryayoḥ ,trivarṇāḥ śyāmarūkṣāntāstathā bhasmāruṇaprabhāḥ.
english: And dreadful halos are observed around both the moon and the sun. They appear three-colored, with dark and rough edges, and also possess an ash-like, reddish glow.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. And dreadful halos are observed around both the moon and the sun. They appear three-colored, with dark and rough edges, and also possess an ash-like, reddish glow.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहव उत्पाता भयशंसिनः ।दृश्यन्तेऽहरहो राजन्हृदयोद्वेगकारकाः ॥६॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahava utpātā bhayaśaṁsinaḥ ,dṛśyante'haraho rājanhṛdayodvegakārakāḥ.
english: O king, these and many other dreadful omens, portending fear, are observed daily, causing distress and anxiety in the heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. O king, these and many other dreadful omens, portending fear, are observed daily, causing distress and anxiety in the heart.

sanskrit: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।शुश्राव वृष्णिचक्रस्य मौसले कदनं कृतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kasyacittvatha kālasya kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śuśrāva vṛṣṇicakrasya mausale kadanaṁ kṛtam.
english: Then, after some time, Yudhishthira, the king of the Kurus, heard about the destruction (kadana) of the Vrishni clan (vṛṣṇicakra) that had occurred during the Mauṣala event.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Then, after some time, Yudhishthira, the king of the Kurus, heard about the destruction (kadana) of the Vrishni clan (vṛṣṇicakra) that had occurred during the Mauṣala event.

sanskrit: विमुक्तं वासुदेवं च श्रुत्वा रामं च पाण्डवः ।समानीयाब्रवीद्भ्रातॄन्किं करिष्याम इत्युत ॥८॥
transliteration: vimuktaṁ vāsudevaṁ ca śrutvā rāmaṁ ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,samānīyābravīdbhrātṝnkiṁ kariṣyāma ityuta.
english: Having heard that Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and Rāma (Balarāma) had been liberated [from their earthly forms], the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) assembled his brothers and asked, 'What shall we do?'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. Having heard that Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa) and Rāma (Balarāma) had been liberated [from their earthly forms], the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) assembled his brothers and asked, 'What shall we do?'

sanskrit: परस्परं समासाद्य ब्रह्मदण्डबलात्कृतान् ।वृष्णीन्विनष्टांस्ते श्रुत्वा व्यथिताः पाण्डवाभवन् ॥९॥
transliteration: parasparaṁ samāsādya brahmadaṇḍabalātkṛtān ,vṛṣṇīnvinaṣṭāṁste śrutvā vyathitāḥ pāṇḍavābhavan.
english: Having heard that the Vṛṣṇis, compelled by the power of a brahmin's curse, had clashed and were destroyed, the Pāṇḍavas became distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Having heard that the Vṛṣṇis, compelled by the power of a brahmin's curse, had clashed and were destroyed, the Pāṇḍavas became distressed.

sanskrit: निधनं वासुदेवस्य समुद्रस्येव शोषणम् ।वीरा न श्रद्दधुस्तस्य विनाशं शार्ङ्गधन्वनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: nidhanaṁ vāsudevasya samudrasyeva śoṣaṇam ,vīrā na śraddadhustasya vināśaṁ śārṅgadhanvanaḥ.
english: The heroes (Pāṇḍavas) did not believe in the demise of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the wielder of the Śārṅga bow, considering his destruction to be as impossible as the drying up of the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. The heroes (Pāṇḍavas) did not believe in the demise of Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the wielder of the Śārṅga bow, considering his destruction to be as impossible as the drying up of the ocean.

sanskrit: मौसलं ते परिश्रुत्य दुःखशोकसमन्विताः ।विषण्णा हतसंकल्पाः पाण्डवाः समुपाविशन् ॥११॥
transliteration: mausalaṁ te pariśrutya duḥkhaśokasamanvitāḥ ,viṣaṇṇā hatasaṁkalpāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ samupāviśan.
english: Upon fully hearing about the Mausala destruction, the Pāṇḍavas, filled with sorrow and grief, became despondent and disheartened, and then sat down.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. Upon fully hearing about the Mausala destruction, the Pāṇḍavas, filled with sorrow and grief, became despondent and disheartened, and then sat down.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कथं विनष्टा भगवन्नन्धका वृष्णिभिः सह ।पश्यतो वासुदेवस्य भोजाश्चैव महारथाः ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kathaṁ vinaṣṭā bhagavannandhakā vṛṣṇibhiḥ saha ,paśyato vāsudevasya bhojāścaiva mahārathāḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: O revered one, how were the Andhakas, along with the Vṛṣṇis and the great warriors (mahārathāḥ) of the Bhojas, destroyed while Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) was watching?
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: O revered one, how were the Andhakas, along with the Vṛṣṇis and the great warriors (mahārathāḥ) of the Bhojas, destroyed while Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) was watching?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।षट्त्रिंशेऽथ ततो वर्षे वृष्णीनामनयो महान् ।अन्योन्यं मुसलैस्ते तु निजघ्नुः कालचोदिताः ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ṣaṭtriṁśe'tha tato varṣe vṛṣṇīnāmanayo mahān ,anyonyaṁ musalaiste tu nijaghnuḥ kālacoditāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, in the thirty-sixth year, a great calamity (anaya) befell the Vṛṣṇis. Driven by time (kāla), they struck one another with maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, in the thirty-sixth year, a great calamity (anaya) befell the Vṛṣṇis. Driven by time (kāla), they struck one another with maces.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।केनानुशप्तास्ते वीराः क्षयं वृष्ण्यन्धका ययुः ।भोजाश्च द्विजवर्य त्वं विस्तरेण वदस्व मे ॥३॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kenānuśaptāste vīrāḥ kṣayaṁ vṛṣṇyandhakā yayuḥ ,bhojāśca dvijavarya tvaṁ vistareṇa vadasva me.
english: Janamejaya said: By whom were those heroes, the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, along with the Bhojas, cursed to destruction? O best of the twice-born (dvija), please tell me in detail.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. Janamejaya said: By whom were those heroes, the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, along with the Bhojas, cursed to destruction? O best of the twice-born (dvija), please tell me in detail.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विश्वामित्रं च कण्वं च नारदं च तपोधनम् ।सारणप्रमुखा वीरा ददृशुर्द्वारकागतान् ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,viśvāmitraṁ ca kaṇvaṁ ca nāradaṁ ca tapodhanam ,sāraṇapramukhā vīrā dadṛśurdvārakāgatān.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: The heroes led by Sāraṇa (Sāraṇapramukhāḥ) saw Viśvāmitra, Kaṇva, and Nārada, who possessed ascetic wealth (tapas), as they arrived in Dvārakā.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: The heroes led by Sāraṇa (Sāraṇapramukhāḥ) saw Viśvāmitra, Kaṇva, and Nārada, who possessed ascetic wealth (tapas), as they arrived in Dvārakā.

sanskrit: ते वै साम्बं पुरस्कृत्य भूषयित्वा स्त्रियं यथा ।अब्रुवन्नुपसंगम्य दैवदण्डनिपीडिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: te vai sāmbaṁ puraskṛtya bhūṣayitvā striyaṁ yathā ,abruvannupasaṁgamya daivadaṇḍanipīḍitāḥ.
english: Afflicted by divine retribution, they indeed approached, having dressed Samba like a woman and placed him before them, and then spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. Afflicted by divine retribution, they indeed approached, having dressed Samba like a woman and placed him before them, and then spoke.

sanskrit: इयं स्त्री पुत्रकामस्य बभ्रोरमिततेजसः ।ऋषयः साधु जानीत किमियं जनयिष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: iyaṁ strī putrakāmasya babhroramitatejasaḥ ,ṛṣayaḥ sādhu jānīta kimiyaṁ janayiṣyati.
english: This woman belongs to Babhru, of immeasurable splendor, who desires a son. O revered sages (muni), please tell us clearly: what will she give birth to?
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. This woman belongs to Babhru, of immeasurable splendor, who desires a son. O revered sages (muni), please tell us clearly: what will she give birth to?

sanskrit: इत्युक्तास्ते तदा राजन्विप्रलम्भप्रधर्षिताः ।प्रत्यब्रुवंस्तान्मुनयो यत्तच्छृणु नराधिप ॥७॥
transliteration: ityuktāste tadā rājanvipralambhapradharṣitāḥ ,pratyabruvaṁstānmunayo yattacchṛṇu narādhipa.
english: O King (rājan), thus addressed and feeling insulted by the deceit, the sages (muni) then replied to them. O ruler of men, hear what they said.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. O King (rājan), thus addressed and feeling insulted by the deceit, the sages (muni) then replied to them. O ruler of men, hear what they said.

sanskrit: वृष्ण्यन्धकविनाशाय मुसलं घोरमायसम् ।वासुदेवस्य दायादः साम्बोऽयं जनयिष्यति ॥८॥
transliteration: vṛṣṇyandhakavināśāya musalaṁ ghoramāyasam ,vāsudevasya dāyādaḥ sāmbo'yaṁ janayiṣyati.
english: This Samba, the descendant of Vasudeva, will give birth to a terrible iron club, destined for the destruction of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. This Samba, the descendant of Vasudeva, will give birth to a terrible iron club, destined for the destruction of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.

sanskrit: येन यूयं सुदुर्वृत्ता नृशंसा जातमन्यवः ।उच्छेत्तारः कुलं कृत्स्नमृते रामजनार्दनौ ॥९॥
transliteration: yena yūyaṁ sudurvṛttā nṛśaṁsā jātamanyavaḥ ,ucchettāraḥ kulaṁ kṛtsnamṛte rāmajanārdanau.
english: Because of this, you, who are very ill-behaved, cruel, and enraged, will be the destroyers of your entire family, with the exception of Rāma and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. Because of this, you, who are very ill-behaved, cruel, and enraged, will be the destroyers of your entire family, with the exception of Rāma and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: समुद्रं यास्यति श्रीमांस्त्यक्त्वा देहं हलायुधः ।जरा कृष्णं महात्मानं शयानं भुवि भेत्स्यति ॥१०॥
transliteration: samudraṁ yāsyati śrīmāṁstyaktvā dehaṁ halāyudhaḥ ,jarā kṛṣṇaṁ mahātmānaṁ śayānaṁ bhuvi bhetsyati.
english: The glorious Halāyudha (Balarāma), abandoning his body, will merge into the ocean. Old age will overcome Kṛṣṇa, the great (ātman), as he lies on the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. The glorious Halāyudha (Balarāma), abandoning his body, will merge into the ocean. Old age will overcome Kṛṣṇa, the great (ātman), as he lies on the earth.

sanskrit: इत्यब्रुवन्त ते राजन्प्रलब्धास्तैर्दुरात्मभिः ।मुनयः क्रोधरक्ताक्षाः समीक्ष्याथ परस्परम् ॥११॥
transliteration: ityabruvanta te rājanpralabdhāstairdurātmabhiḥ ,munayaḥ krodharaktākṣāḥ samīkṣyātha parasparam.
english: O King, the sages, who had been mocked by those wicked-minded (persons), spoke thus, after looking at each other with eyes red from anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. O King, the sages, who had been mocked by those wicked-minded (persons), spoke thus, after looking at each other with eyes red from anger.

sanskrit: तथोक्त्वा मुनयस्तेतु ततः केशवमभ्ययुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tathoktvā munayaste tutataḥ keśavamabhyayuḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, those sages then approached Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. Having spoken thus, those sages then approached Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्तदा वृष्णीञ्श्रुत्वैवं मधुसूदनः ।अन्तज्ञो मतिमांस्तस्य भवितव्यं तथेति तान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: athābravīttadā vṛṣṇīñśrutvaivaṁ madhusūdanaḥ ,antajño matimāṁstasya bhavitavyaṁ tatheti tān.
english: Then, having heard this, the sagacious Krishna (madhusūdana), who understood the ultimate outcome, spoke to those Vrishnis, acknowledging that "such must be the destiny (bhavitavya)."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Then, having heard this, the sagacious Krishna (madhusūdana), who understood the ultimate outcome, spoke to those Vrishnis, acknowledging that "such must be the destiny (bhavitavya)."

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशः प्रविवेश पुनर्गृहान् ।कृतान्तमन्यथा नैच्छत्कर्तुं स जगतः प्रभुः ॥१४॥
transliteration: evamuktvā hṛṣīkeśaḥ praviveśa punargṛhān ,kṛtāntamanyathā naicchatkartuṁ sa jagataḥ prabhuḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Krishna (hṛṣīkeśa) re-entered his dwellings. He, the lord of the world, had no desire to change what was destined (kṛtānta).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. Having spoken thus, Krishna (hṛṣīkeśa) re-entered his dwellings. He, the lord of the world, had no desire to change what was destined (kṛtānta).

sanskrit: श्वोभूतेऽथ ततः साम्बो मुसलं तदसूत वै ।वृष्ण्यन्धकविनाशाय किंकरप्रतिमं महत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: śvobhūte'tha tataḥ sāmbo musalaṁ tadasūta vai ,vṛṣṇyandhakavināśāya kiṁkarapratimaṁ mahat.
english: Then, on the following day, Samba indeed produced that great club (musala), formidable and resembling a mace of a divine attendant (kiṅkarapratima), for the annihilation of the Vrishnis and Andhakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. Then, on the following day, Samba indeed produced that great club (musala), formidable and resembling a mace of a divine attendant (kiṅkarapratima), for the annihilation of the Vrishnis and Andhakas.

sanskrit: प्रसूतं शापजं घोरं तच्च राज्ञे न्यवेदयन् ।विषण्णरूपस्तद्राजा सूक्ष्मं चूर्णमकारयत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: prasūtaṁ śāpajaṁ ghoraṁ tacca rājñe nyavedayan ,viṣaṇṇarūpastadrājā sūkṣmaṁ cūrṇamakārayat.
english: And they reported that terrible, curse-born (śāpaja) product to the king. That king, appearing dejected, then had it ground into a fine powder.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. And they reported that terrible, curse-born (śāpaja) product to the king. That king, appearing dejected, then had it ground into a fine powder.

sanskrit: प्राक्षिपन्सागरे तच्च पुरुषा राजशासनात् ।अघोषयंश्च नगरे वचनादाहुकस्य च ॥१७॥
transliteration: prākṣipansāgare tacca puruṣā rājaśāsanāt ,aghoṣayaṁśca nagare vacanādāhukasya ca.
english: And men, by the king's command, threw that into the ocean. And they also proclaimed in the city on the instruction of Āhuka.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. And men, by the king's command, threw that into the ocean. And they also proclaimed in the city on the instruction of Āhuka.

sanskrit: अद्य प्रभृति सर्वेषु वृष्ण्यन्धकगृहेष्विह ।सुरासवो न कर्तव्यः सर्वैर्नगरवासिभिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: adya prabhṛti sarveṣu vṛṣṇyandhakagṛheṣviha ,surāsavo na kartavyaḥ sarvairnagaravāsibhiḥ.
english: From today onwards, in all homes of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas here, fermented liquor (surāsava) should not be made or consumed by any of the city residents.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. From today onwards, in all homes of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas here, fermented liquor (surāsava) should not be made or consumed by any of the city residents.

sanskrit: यश्च नोऽविदितं कुर्यात्पेयं कश्चिन्नरः क्वचित् ।जीवन्स शूलमारोहेत्स्वयं कृत्वा सबान्धवः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yaśca no'viditaṁ kuryātpeyaṁ kaścinnaraḥ kvacit ,jīvansa śūlamārohetsvayaṁ kṛtvā sabāndhavaḥ.
english: And any man who, anywhere, without our knowledge, prepares any intoxicating drink (peyam), he himself, while still alive, shall be impaled on a stake along with his relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. And any man who, anywhere, without our knowledge, prepares any intoxicating drink (peyam), he himself, while still alive, shall be impaled on a stake along with his relatives.

sanskrit: ततो राजभयात्सर्वे नियमं चक्रिरे तदा ।नराः शासनमाज्ञाय तस्य राज्ञो महात्मनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato rājabhayātsarve niyamaṁ cakrire tadā ,narāḥ śāsanamājñāya tasya rājño mahātmanaḥ.
english: Then, out of fear of the king, all the people, understanding the command of that noble (mahātman) king, established this regulation at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. Then, out of fear of the king, all the people, understanding the command of that noble (mahātman) king, established this regulation at that time.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं प्रयतमानानां वृष्णीनामन्धकैः सह ।कालो गृहाणि सर्वेषां परिचक्राम नित्यशः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ prayatamānānāṁ vṛṣṇīnāmandhakaiḥ saha ,kālo gṛhāṇi sarveṣāṁ paricakrāma nityaśaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: As the Vrishnis and Andhakas were thus engaged in their efforts, time (kāla) constantly circled the homes of all of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: As the Vrishnis and Andhakas were thus engaged in their efforts, time (kāla) constantly circled the homes of all of them.

sanskrit: करालो विकटो मुण्डः पुरुषः कृष्णपिङ्गलः ।गृहाण्यवेक्ष्य वृष्णीनां नादृश्यत पुनः क्वचित् ॥२॥
transliteration: karālo vikaṭo muṇḍaḥ puruṣaḥ kṛṣṇapiṅgalaḥ ,gṛhāṇyavekṣya vṛṣṇīnāṁ nādṛśyata punaḥ kvacit.
english: A formidable, huge, bald-headed, blackish-brown man, after having observed the homes of the Vrishnis, was then nowhere to be seen again.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. A formidable, huge, bald-headed, blackish-brown man, after having observed the homes of the Vrishnis, was then nowhere to be seen again.

sanskrit: उत्पेदिरे महावाता दारुणाश्चा दिने दिने ।वृष्ण्यन्धकविनाशाय बहवो रोमहर्षणाः ॥३॥
transliteration: utpedire mahāvātā dāruṇāścā dine dine ,vṛṣṇyandhakavināśāya bahavo romaharṣaṇāḥ.
english: Great, dreadful, and hair-raising winds arose day by day for the annihilation of the Vrishnis and Andhakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. Great, dreadful, and hair-raising winds arose day by day for the annihilation of the Vrishnis and Andhakas.

sanskrit: विवृद्धमूषका रथ्या विभिन्नमणिकास्तथा ।चीचीकूचीति वाश्यन्त्यः सारिका वृष्णिवेश्मसु ।नोपशाम्यति शब्दश्च स दिवारात्रमेव हि ॥४॥
transliteration: vivṛddhamūṣakā rathyā vibhinnamaṇikāstathā ,cīcīkūcīti vāśyantyaḥ sārikā vṛṣṇiveśmasu ,nopaśāmyati śabdaśca sa divārātrameva hi.
english: Rampant street-dwelling mice and broken pitchers (maṇikā) [were seen everywhere]; and mynah birds chirped "chee-chee-koo-chee" in the Vrishni residences. That sound, indeed, did not cease day and night.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. Rampant street-dwelling mice and broken pitchers (maṇikā) [were seen everywhere]; and mynah birds chirped "chee-chee-koo-chee" in the Vrishni residences. That sound, indeed, did not cease day and night.

sanskrit: अनुकुर्वन्नुलूकानां सारसा विरुतं तथा ।अजाः शिवानां च रुतमन्वकुर्वत भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: anukurvannulūkānāṁ sārasā virutaṁ tathā ,ajāḥ śivānāṁ ca rutamanvakurvata bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the cranes mimicked the cries of owls, and similarly, goats mimicked the cries of jackals.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. O Bhārata, the cranes mimicked the cries of owls, and similarly, goats mimicked the cries of jackals.

sanskrit: पाण्डुरा रक्तपादाश्च विहगाः कालचोदिताः ।वृष्ण्यन्धकानां गेहेषु कपोता व्यचरंस्तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: pāṇḍurā raktapādāśca vihagāḥ kālacoditāḥ ,vṛṣṇyandhakānāṁ geheṣu kapotā vyacaraṁstadā.
english: White-bodied, red-footed birds, driven by fate (kāla), and pigeons then roamed about in the houses of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. White-bodied, red-footed birds, driven by fate (kāla), and pigeons then roamed about in the houses of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.

sanskrit: व्यजायन्त खरा गोषु करभाश्वतरीषु च ।शुनीष्वपि बिडालाश्च मूषका नकुलीषु च ॥७॥
transliteration: vyajāyanta kharā goṣu karabhāśvatarīṣu ca ,śunīṣvapi biḍālāśca mūṣakā nakulīṣu ca.
english: Donkeys were born among cows, camels among she-mules, cats among female dogs, and mice among mongooses.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. Donkeys were born among cows, camels among she-mules, cats among female dogs, and mice among mongooses.

sanskrit: नापत्रपन्त पापानि कुर्वन्तो वृष्णयस्तदा ।प्राद्विषन्ब्राह्मणांश्चापि पितॄन्देवांस्तथैव च ॥८॥
transliteration: nāpatrapanta pāpāni kurvanto vṛṣṇayastadā ,prādviṣanbrāhmaṇāṁścāpi pitṝndevāṁstathaiva ca.
english: At that time, the Vṛṣṇis, performing sinful acts, felt no shame. Moreover, they hated Brahmins, ancestors, and likewise the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. At that time, the Vṛṣṇis, performing sinful acts, felt no shame. Moreover, they hated Brahmins, ancestors, and likewise the gods.

sanskrit: गुरूंश्चाप्यवमन्यन्त न तु रामजनार्दनौ ।पत्न्यः पतीन्व्युच्चरन्त पत्नीश्च पतयस्तथा ॥९॥
transliteration: gurūṁścāpyavamanyanta na tu rāmajanārdanau ,patnyaḥ patīnvyuccaranta patnīśca patayastathā.
english: People even disrespected their spiritual teachers (guru), but not Rāma and Janārdana. Wives abandoned their husbands, and husbands likewise abandoned their wives.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. People even disrespected their spiritual teachers (guru), but not Rāma and Janārdana. Wives abandoned their husbands, and husbands likewise abandoned their wives.

sanskrit: विभावसुः प्रज्वलितो वामं विपरिवर्तते ।नीललोहितमाञ्जिष्ठा विसृजन्नर्चिषः पृथक् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vibhāvasuḥ prajvalito vāmaṁ viparivartate ,nīlalohitamāñjiṣṭhā visṛjannarciṣaḥ pṛthak.
english: The blazing fire (Agni) turned to the left (clockwise), emitting distinct flames of blue, red, and madder colors.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. The blazing fire (Agni) turned to the left (clockwise), emitting distinct flames of blue, red, and madder colors.

sanskrit: उदयास्तमने नित्यं पुर्यां तस्यां दिवाकरः ।व्यदृश्यतासकृत्पुंभिः कबन्धैः परिवारितः ॥११॥
transliteration: udayāstamane nityaṁ puryāṁ tasyāṁ divākaraḥ ,vyadṛśyatāsakṛtpuṁbhiḥ kabandhaiḥ parivāritaḥ.
english: In that city, at sunrise and sunset, the sun was constantly and repeatedly seen by men, surrounded by headless torsos (kabandha).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. In that city, at sunrise and sunset, the sun was constantly and repeatedly seen by men, surrounded by headless torsos (kabandha).

sanskrit: महानसेषु सिद्धेऽन्ने संस्कृतेऽतीव भारत ।आहार्यमाणे कृमयो व्यदृश्यन्त नराधिप ॥१२॥
transliteration: mahānaseṣu siddhe'nne saṁskṛte'tīva bhārata ,āhāryamāṇe kṛmayo vyadṛśyanta narādhipa.
english: O Bhārata, O king of men, in the great kitchens, even in the very well-prepared and cooked food, as it was being served, worms were seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. O Bhārata, O king of men, in the great kitchens, even in the very well-prepared and cooked food, as it was being served, worms were seen.

sanskrit: पुण्याहे वाच्यमाने च जपत्सु च महात्मसु ।अभिधावन्तः श्रूयन्ते न चादृश्यत कश्चन ॥१३॥
transliteration: puṇyāhe vācyamāne ca japatsu ca mahātmasu ,abhidhāvantaḥ śrūyante na cādṛśyata kaścana.
english: While auspicious day rites were being announced and great souls were chanting, sounds of rushing were heard, yet no one was actually seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. While auspicious day rites were being announced and great souls were chanting, sounds of rushing were heard, yet no one was actually seen.

sanskrit: परस्परं च नक्षत्रं हन्यमानं पुनः पुनः ।ग्रहैरपश्यन्सर्वे ते नात्मनस्तु कथंचन ॥१४॥
transliteration: parasparaṁ ca nakṣatraṁ hanyamānaṁ punaḥ punaḥ ,grahairapaśyansarve te nātmanastu kathaṁcana.
english: They all observed the constellations being repeatedly struck by planets, but by no means did they see their own stars (ātman) being afflicted.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. They all observed the constellations being repeatedly struck by planets, but by no means did they see their own stars (ātman) being afflicted.

sanskrit: नदन्तं पाञ्चजन्यं च वृष्ण्यन्धकनिवेशने ।समन्तात्प्रत्यवाश्यन्त रासभा दारुणस्वराः ॥१५॥
transliteration: nadantaṁ pāñcajanyaṁ ca vṛṣṇyandhakaniveśane ,samantātpratyavāśyanta rāsabhā dāruṇasvarāḥ.
english: In the settlements of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, as the Pāñcajanya conch roared, dreadful-voiced donkeys brayed back from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. In the settlements of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, as the Pāñcajanya conch roared, dreadful-voiced donkeys brayed back from all sides.

sanskrit: एवं पश्यन्हृषीकेशः संप्राप्तं कालपर्ययम् ।त्रयोदश्याममावास्यां तां दृष्ट्वा प्राब्रवीदिदम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ paśyanhṛṣīkeśaḥ saṁprāptaṁ kālaparyayam ,trayodaśyāmamāvāsyāṁ tāṁ dṛṣṭvā prābravīdidam.
english: Thus, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), observing the arrival of this critical juncture of time, and having seen that particular thirteenth day which was also a new moon (amāvāsyā), spoke the following words.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Thus, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), observing the arrival of this critical juncture of time, and having seen that particular thirteenth day which was also a new moon (amāvāsyā), spoke the following words.

sanskrit: चतुर्दशी पञ्चदशी कृतेयं राहुणा पुनः ।तदा च भारते युद्धे प्राप्ता चाद्य क्षयाय नः ॥१७॥
transliteration: caturdaśī pañcadaśī kṛteyaṁ rāhuṇā punaḥ ,tadā ca bhārate yuddhe prāptā cādya kṣayāya naḥ.
english: The fourteenth and fifteenth (tithis) have been made into one by Rāhu again. And that very day, when the war in Bhārata took place, has now arrived for our annihilation.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. The fourteenth and fifteenth (tithis) have been made into one by Rāhu again. And that very day, when the war in Bhārata took place, has now arrived for our annihilation.

sanskrit: विमृशन्नेव कालं तं परिचिन्त्य जनार्दनः ।मेने प्राप्तं स षट्त्रिंशं वर्षं वै केशिसूदनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: vimṛśanneva kālaṁ taṁ paricintya janārdanaḥ ,mene prāptaṁ sa ṣaṭtriṁśaṁ varṣaṁ vai keśisūdanaḥ.
english: Lord Janārdana, the slayer of Keśin, pondering and reflecting on that time, realized that the thirty-sixth year had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. Lord Janārdana, the slayer of Keśin, pondering and reflecting on that time, realized that the thirty-sixth year had arrived.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्ता गान्धारी हतबान्धवा ।यदनुव्याजहारार्ता तदिदं समुपागतम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: putraśokābhisaṁtaptā gāndhārī hatabāndhavā ,yadanuvyājahārārtā tadidaṁ samupāgatam.
english: Gāndhārī, scorched by the grief for her sons and bereft of kinsmen, had lamented in her anguish. That very thing which she had thus declared has now come to pass.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. Gāndhārī, scorched by the grief for her sons and bereft of kinsmen, had lamented in her anguish. That very thing which she had thus declared has now come to pass.

sanskrit: इदं च तदनुप्राप्तमब्रवीद्यद्युधिष्ठिरः ।पुरा व्यूढेष्वनीकेषु दृष्ट्वोत्पातान्सुदारुणान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca tadanuprāptamabravīdyadyudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,purā vyūḍheṣvanīkeṣu dṛṣṭvotpātānsudāruṇān.
english: And this has also come to pass, what Yudhiṣṭhira said previously, having seen exceedingly terrible omens when the armies were arrayed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. And this has also come to pass, what Yudhiṣṭhira said previously, having seen exceedingly terrible omens when the armies were arrayed.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा वासुदेवस्तु चिकीर्षन्सत्यमेव तत् ।आज्ञापयामास तदा तीर्थयात्रामरिंदम ॥२१॥
transliteration: ityuktvā vāsudevastu cikīrṣansatyameva tat ,ājñāpayāmāsa tadā tīrthayātrāmariṁdama.
english: Having spoken thus, Vāsudeva, intending to make that (prophecy) come true, then commanded a pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā), O subduer of foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. Having spoken thus, Vāsudeva, intending to make that (prophecy) come true, then commanded a pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā), O subduer of foes.

sanskrit: अघोषयन्त पुरुषास्तत्र केशवशासनात् ।तीर्थयात्रा समुद्रे वः कार्येति पुरुषर्षभाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: aghoṣayanta puruṣāstatra keśavaśāsanāt ,tīrthayātrā samudre vaḥ kāryeti puruṣarṣabhāḥ.
english: There, by Keśava's command, men proclaimed: 'A pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā) to the sea must be undertaken by you, O best among men!'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. There, by Keśava's command, men proclaimed: 'A pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā) to the sea must be undertaken by you, O best among men!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।काली स्त्री पाण्डुरैर्दन्तैः प्रविश्य हसती निशि ।स्त्रियः स्वप्नेषु मुष्णन्ती द्वारकां परिधावति ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kālī strī pāṇḍurairdantaiḥ praviśya hasatī niśi ,striyaḥ svapneṣu muṣṇantī dvārakāṁ paridhāvati.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: A dark woman with pale teeth, laughing, enters (homes) at night and roams throughout Dvārakā, stealing from women in their dreams.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: A dark woman with pale teeth, laughing, enters (homes) at night and roams throughout Dvārakā, stealing from women in their dreams.

sanskrit: अलंकाराश्च छत्रं च ध्वजाश्च कवचानि च ।ह्रियमाणान्यदृश्यन्त रक्षोभिः सुभयानकैः ॥२॥
transliteration: alaṁkārāśca chatraṁ ca dhvajāśca kavacāni ca ,hriyamāṇānyadṛśyanta rakṣobhiḥ subhayānakaiḥ.
english: Ornaments, parasols, banners, and armors were seen being carried away by very terrifying rākṣasas (demons).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. Ornaments, parasols, banners, and armors were seen being carried away by very terrifying rākṣasas (demons).

sanskrit: तच्चाग्निदत्तं कृष्णस्य वज्रनाभमयस्मयम् ।दिवमाचक्रमे चक्रं वृष्णीनां पश्यतां तदा ॥३॥
transliteration: taccāgnidattaṁ kṛṣṇasya vajranābhamayasmayam ,divamācakrame cakraṁ vṛṣṇīnāṁ paśyatāṁ tadā.
english: Then, that discus (cakra), given by Agni, belonging to Kṛṣṇa, which had a thunderbolt-like hub and was made of iron, ascended to the sky as the Vṛṣṇis watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. Then, that discus (cakra), given by Agni, belonging to Kṛṣṇa, which had a thunderbolt-like hub and was made of iron, ascended to the sky as the Vṛṣṇis watched.

sanskrit: युक्तं रथं दिव्यमादित्यवर्णं हयाहरन्पश्यतो दारुकस्य ।ते सागरस्योपरिष्टादवर्तन्मनोजवाश्चतुरो वाजिमुख्याः ॥४॥
transliteration: yuktaṁ rathaṁ divyamādityavarṇaṁ; hayāharanpaśyato dārukasya ,te sāgarasyopariṣṭādavarta;nmanojavāścaturo vājimukhyāḥ.
english: As Dāruka watched, the four chief horses, swift as thought, carried away the yoked, divine, sun-colored chariot. They then moved above the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. As Dāruka watched, the four chief horses, swift as thought, carried away the yoked, divine, sun-colored chariot. They then moved above the ocean.

sanskrit: तालः सुपर्णश्च महाध्वजौ तौ सुपूजितौ रामजनार्दनाभ्याम् ।उच्चैर्जह्रुरप्सरसो दिवानिशं वाचश्चोचुर्गम्यतां तीर्थयात्रा ॥५॥
transliteration: tālaḥ suparṇaśca mahādhvajau tau; supūjitau rāmajanārdanābhyām ,uccairjahrurapsaraso divāniśaṁ; vācaścocurgamyatāṁ tīrthayātrā.
english: The two great banners, namely the palm tree and Garuḍa, which were well-honored by Rāma and Janārdana, and the Apsaras, day and night, loudly announced and declared, 'Let the sacred journey (tīrthayātrā) be undertaken!'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. The two great banners, namely the palm tree and Garuḍa, which were well-honored by Rāma and Janārdana, and the Apsaras, day and night, loudly announced and declared, 'Let the sacred journey (tīrthayātrā) be undertaken!'

sanskrit: ततो जिगमिषन्तस्ते वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथाः ।सान्तःपुरास्तदा तीर्थयात्रामैच्छन्नरर्षभाः ॥६॥
transliteration: tato jigamiṣantaste vṛṣṇyandhakamahārathāḥ ,sāntaḥpurāstadā tīrthayātrāmaicchannararṣabhāḥ.
english: Then, those great warriors, the foremost among men from the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans, wishing to embark, desired a sacred journey (tīrthayātrā) at that time, accompanied by their inner chambers (women).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. Then, those great warriors, the foremost among men from the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans, wishing to embark, desired a sacred journey (tīrthayātrā) at that time, accompanied by their inner chambers (women).

sanskrit: ततो भोज्यं च भक्ष्यं च पेयं चान्धकवृष्णयः ।बहु नानाविधं चक्रुर्मद्यं मांसमनेकशः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato bhojyaṁ ca bhakṣyaṁ ca peyaṁ cāndhakavṛṣṇayaḥ ,bahu nānāvidhaṁ cakrurmadyaṁ māṁsamanekaśaḥ.
english: Then the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis prepared abundant quantities of various foods, eatables, drinks, and many kinds of liquor and meat.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. Then the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis prepared abundant quantities of various foods, eatables, drinks, and many kinds of liquor and meat.

sanskrit: ततः सीधुषु सक्ताश्च निर्ययुर्नगराद्बहिः ।यानैरश्वैर्गजैश्चैव श्रीमन्तस्तिग्मतेजसः ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sīdhuṣu saktāśca niryayurnagarādbahiḥ ,yānairaśvairgajaiścaiva śrīmantastigmatejasaḥ.
english: Then, having become engrossed in strong drinks, the prosperous and fiercely energetic (Yādavas) exited the city by means of vehicles, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. Then, having become engrossed in strong drinks, the prosperous and fiercely energetic (Yādavas) exited the city by means of vehicles, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: ततः प्रभासे न्यवसन्यथोद्देशं यथागृहम् ।प्रभूतभक्ष्यपेयास्ते सदारा यादवास्तदा ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ prabhāse nyavasanyathoddeśaṁ yathāgṛham ,prabhūtabhakṣyapeyāste sadārā yādavāstadā.
english: Then, at that time, those Yadavas, accompanied by their wives and possessing abundant food and drink, settled in Prabhāsa, each in their designated spots and respective lodgings.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. Then, at that time, those Yadavas, accompanied by their wives and possessing abundant food and drink, settled in Prabhāsa, each in their designated spots and respective lodgings.

sanskrit: निविष्टांस्तान्निशम्याथ समुद्रान्ते स योगवित् ।जगामामन्त्र्य तान्वीरानुद्धवोऽर्थविशारदः ॥१०॥
transliteration: niviṣṭāṁstānniśamyātha samudrānte sa yogavit ,jagāmāmantrya tānvīrānuddhavo'rthaviśāradaḥ.
english: Then, having observed those heroes settled at the seashore, Uddhava, that knower of (yoga) and expert in practical affairs, took his leave of them and departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Then, having observed those heroes settled at the seashore, Uddhava, that knower of (yoga) and expert in practical affairs, took his leave of them and departed.

sanskrit: तं प्रस्थितं महात्मानमभिवाद्य कृताञ्जलिम् ।जानन्विनाशं वृष्णीनां नैच्छद्वारयितुं हरिः ॥११॥
transliteration: taṁ prasthitaṁ mahātmānamabhivādya kṛtāñjalim ,jānanvināśaṁ vṛṣṇīnāṁ naicchadvārayituṁ hariḥ.
english: Knowing the impending destruction of the Vṛṣṇis, Hari (Kṛṣṇa) did not wish to stop that great soul, who had departed after saluting Him with folded hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Knowing the impending destruction of the Vṛṣṇis, Hari (Kṛṣṇa) did not wish to stop that great soul, who had departed after saluting Him with folded hands.

sanskrit: ततः कालपरीतास्ते वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथाः ।अपश्यन्नुद्धवं यान्तं तेजसावृत्य रोदसी ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ kālaparītāste vṛṣṇyandhakamahārathāḥ ,apaśyannuddhavaṁ yāntaṁ tejasāvṛtya rodasī.
english: Then, those great charioteers of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, overcome by destiny, saw Uddhava departing, enveloping heaven and earth with his radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. Then, those great charioteers of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, overcome by destiny, saw Uddhava departing, enveloping heaven and earth with his radiance.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणार्थेषु यत्सिद्धमन्नं तेषां महात्मनाम् ।तद्वानरेभ्यः प्रददुः सुरागन्धसमन्वितम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇārtheṣu yatsiddhamannaṁ teṣāṁ mahātmanām ,tadvānarebhyaḥ pradaduḥ surāgandhasamanvitam.
english: That food, which was prepared for the Brahmins and intended for those great souls, they gave to monkeys, permeated with the smell of liquor.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. That food, which was prepared for the Brahmins and intended for those great souls, they gave to monkeys, permeated with the smell of liquor.

sanskrit: ततस्तूर्यशताकीर्णं नटनर्तकसंकुलम् ।प्रावर्तत महापानं प्रभासे तिग्मतेजसाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastūryaśatākīrṇaṁ naṭanartakasaṁkulam ,prāvartata mahāpānaṁ prabhāse tigmatejasām.
english: Then, in Prabhāsa, a great drinking bout for those of fierce splendor commenced, filled with hundreds of musical instruments and crowded with actors and dancers.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. Then, in Prabhāsa, a great drinking bout for those of fierce splendor commenced, filled with hundreds of musical instruments and crowded with actors and dancers.

sanskrit: कृष्णस्य संनिधौ रामः सहितः कृतवर्मणा ।अपिबद्युयुधानश्च गदो बभ्रुस्तथैव च ॥१५॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇasya saṁnidhau rāmaḥ sahitaḥ kṛtavarmaṇā ,apibadyuyudhānaśca gado babhrustathaiva ca.
english: In Krishna's presence, Balarama, accompanied by Kritavarman, drank, as did Yuyudhana, Gada, and Babhru.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. In Krishna's presence, Balarama, accompanied by Kritavarman, drank, as did Yuyudhana, Gada, and Babhru.

sanskrit: ततः परिषदो मध्ये युयुधानो मदोत्कटः ।अब्रवीत्कृतवर्माणमवहस्यावमन्य च ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ pariṣado madhye yuyudhāno madotkaṭaḥ ,abravītkṛtavarmāṇamavahasyāvamanya ca.
english: Then, in the midst of the assembly, Yuyudhana, arrogant with intoxication, spoke to Kritavarman, having laughed at and insulted him.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. Then, in the midst of the assembly, Yuyudhana, arrogant with intoxication, spoke to Kritavarman, having laughed at and insulted him.

sanskrit: कः क्षत्रियो मन्यमानः सुप्तान्हन्यान्मृतानिव ।न तन्मृष्यन्ति हार्दिक्य यादवा यत्त्वया कृतम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: kaḥ kṣatriyo manyamānaḥ suptānhanyānmṛtāniva ,na tanmṛṣyanti hārdikya yādavā yattvayā kṛtam.
english: What warrior (kṣatriya), considering himself (a kṣatriya), would kill those who are asleep as if they were dead? O Hardikya (Kritavarman), the Yadavas will not tolerate that which was done by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. What warrior (kṣatriya), considering himself (a kṣatriya), would kill those who are asleep as if they were dead? O Hardikya (Kritavarman), the Yadavas will not tolerate that which was done by you.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते युयुधानेन पूजयामास तद्वचः ।प्रद्युम्नो रथिनां श्रेष्ठो हार्दिक्यमवमन्य च ॥१८॥
transliteration: ityukte yuyudhānena pūjayāmāsa tadvacaḥ ,pradyumno rathināṁ śreṣṭho hārdikyamavamanya ca.
english: When this was said by Yuyudhana, Pradyumna, the foremost of charioteers, honored those words, having also insulted Hardikya (Kritavarman).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. When this was said by Yuyudhana, Pradyumna, the foremost of charioteers, honored those words, having also insulted Hardikya (Kritavarman).

sanskrit: ततः परमसंक्रुद्धः कृतवर्मा तमब्रवीत् ।निर्दिशन्निव सावज्ञं तदा सव्येन पाणिना ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ paramasaṁkruddhaḥ kṛtavarmā tamabravīt ,nirdiśanniva sāvajñaṁ tadā savyena pāṇinā.
english: Then, Kṛtavarmā, extremely enraged, spoke to him, pointing at him disdainfully with his left hand at that moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. Then, Kṛtavarmā, extremely enraged, spoke to him, pointing at him disdainfully with his left hand at that moment.

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवाश्छिन्नबाहुर्युद्धे प्रायगतस्त्वया ।वधेन सुनृशंसेन कथं वीरेण पातितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhūriśravāśchinnabāhuryuddhe prāyagatastvayā ,vadhena sunṛśaṁsena kathaṁ vīreṇa pātitaḥ.
english: How was Bhūriśravas, with his arm severed and undertaking a fast unto death in battle, struck down so cruelly by you, a hero?
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. How was Bhūriśravas, with his arm severed and undertaking a fast unto death in battle, struck down so cruelly by you, a hero?

sanskrit: इति तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा केशवः परवीरहा ।तिर्यक्सरोषया दृष्ट्या वीक्षां चक्रे स मन्युमान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: iti tasya vacaḥ śrutvā keśavaḥ paravīrahā ,tiryaksaroṣayā dṛṣṭyā vīkṣāṁ cakre sa manyumān.
english: Having heard his words, Keśava, the slayer of enemy heroes, who was filled with indignation, cast an angry, sidelong glance.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. Having heard his words, Keśava, the slayer of enemy heroes, who was filled with indignation, cast an angry, sidelong glance.

sanskrit: मणिः स्यमन्तकश्चैव यः स सत्राजितोऽभवत् ।तां कथां स्मारयामास सात्यकिर्मधुसूदनम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: maṇiḥ syamantakaścaiva yaḥ sa satrājito'bhavat ,tāṁ kathāṁ smārayāmāsa sātyakirmadhusūdanam.
english: Sātyaki reminded Madhusūdana (Krishna) of that story about the Syamantaka gem, which had belonged to Satrājit.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. Sātyaki reminded Madhusūdana (Krishna) of that story about the Syamantaka gem, which had belonged to Satrājit.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा केशवस्याङ्कमगमद्रुदती तदा ।सत्यभामा प्रकुपिता कोपयन्ती जनार्दनम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā keśavasyāṅkamagamadrudatī tadā ,satyabhāmā prakupitā kopayantī janārdanam.
english: Having heard that, Satyabhāmā, extremely enraged and weeping, then went to Keśava's lap, thereby further provoking Janārdana.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. Having heard that, Satyabhāmā, extremely enraged and weeping, then went to Keśava's lap, thereby further provoking Janārdana.

sanskrit: तत उत्थाय सक्रोधः सात्यकिर्वाक्यमब्रवीत् ।पञ्चानां द्रौपदेयानां धृष्टद्युम्नशिखण्डिनोः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tata utthāya sakrodhaḥ sātyakirvākyamabravīt ,pañcānāṁ draupadeyānāṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaśikhaṇḍinoḥ.
english: Thereupon, Sātyaki, filled with anger, rose and spoke: 'Regarding the five sons of Draupadī, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin...'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. Thereupon, Sātyaki, filled with anger, rose and spoke: 'Regarding the five sons of Draupadī, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin...'

sanskrit: एष गच्छामि पदवीं सत्येन च तथा शपे ।सौप्तिके ये च निहताः सुप्तानेन दुरात्मना ॥२५॥
transliteration: eṣa gacchāmi padavīṁ satyena ca tathā śape ,sauptike ye ca nihatāḥ suptānena durātmanā.
english: I shall pursue (their avenging) course, and I thus swear by the truth (satya), concerning those who were slain while sleeping by this wicked person in the (episode of) Sauptika.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. I shall pursue (their avenging) course, and I thus swear by the truth (satya), concerning those who were slain while sleeping by this wicked person in the (episode of) Sauptika.

sanskrit: द्रोणपुत्रसहायेन पापेन कृतवर्मणा ।समाप्तमायुरस्याद्य यशश्चापि सुमध्यमे ॥२६॥
transliteration: droṇaputrasahāyena pāpena kṛtavarmaṇā ,samāptamāyurasyādya yaśaścāpi sumadhyame.
english: O slender-waisted one, by the sinful Kṛtavarman, who was an accomplice of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), his life is ended today, and also his fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. O slender-waisted one, by the sinful Kṛtavarman, who was an accomplice of Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), his life is ended today, and also his fame.

sanskrit: इतीदमुक्त्वा खड्गेन केशवस्य समीपतः ।अभिद्रुत्य शिरः क्रुद्धश्चिच्छेद कृतवर्मणः ॥२७॥
transliteration: itīdamuktvā khaḍgena keśavasya samīpataḥ ,abhidrutya śiraḥ kruddhaściccheda kṛtavarmaṇaḥ.
english: Having said this, enraged, he rushed with a sword near Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and severed the head of Kṛtavarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. Having said this, enraged, he rushed with a sword near Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) and severed the head of Kṛtavarman.

sanskrit: तथान्यानपि निघ्नन्तं युयुधानं समन्ततः ।अभ्यधावद्धृषीकेशो विनिवारयिषुस्तदा ॥२८॥
transliteration: tathānyānapi nighnantaṁ yuyudhānaṁ samantataḥ ,abhyadhāvaddhṛṣīkeśo vinivārayiṣustadā.
english: Then, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), wishing to stop him, rushed towards Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), who was similarly striking others all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 28. Then, Hṛṣīkeśa (Kṛṣṇa), wishing to stop him, rushed towards Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), who was similarly striking others all around.

sanskrit: एकीभूतास्ततः सर्वे कालपर्यायचोदिताः ।भोजान्धका महाराज शैनेयं पर्यवारयन् ॥२९॥
transliteration: ekībhūtāstataḥ sarve kālaparyāyacoditāḥ ,bhojāndhakā mahārāja śaineyaṁ paryavārayan.
english: O great king (mahārāja), then all the Bhojas and Andhakas, united and impelled by the turn of time, surrounded Sātyaki (Śaineya).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 29. O great king (mahārāja), then all the Bhojas and Andhakas, united and impelled by the turn of time, surrounded Sātyaki (Śaineya).

sanskrit: तान्दृष्ट्वा पततस्तूर्णमभिक्रुद्धाञ्जनार्दनः ।न चुक्रोध महातेजा जानन्कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tāndṛṣṭvā patatastūrṇamabhikruddhāñjanārdanaḥ ,na cukrodha mahātejā jānankālasya paryayam.
english: Seeing them falling swiftly and greatly enraged, the greatly effulgent Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) did not become angry, knowing the turning point of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 30. Seeing them falling swiftly and greatly enraged, the greatly effulgent Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) did not become angry, knowing the turning point of time.

sanskrit: ते तु पानमदाविष्टाश्चोदिताश्चैव मन्युना ।युयुधानमथाभ्यघ्नन्नुच्छिष्टैर्भाजनैस्तदा ॥३१॥
transliteration: te tu pānamadāviṣṭāścoditāścaiva manyunā ,yuyudhānamathābhyaghnannucchiṣṭairbhājanaistadā.
english: Intoxicated by drink and incited by anger, they then struck Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki) with leftover dishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 31. Intoxicated by drink and incited by anger, they then struck Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki) with leftover dishes.

sanskrit: हन्यमाने तु शैनेये क्रुद्धो रुक्मिणिनन्दनः ।तदन्तरमुपाधावन्मोक्षयिष्यञ्शिनेः सुतम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: hanyamāne tu śaineye kruddho rukmiṇinandanaḥ ,tadantaramupādhāvanmokṣayiṣyañśineḥ sutam.
english: But while Śaineya (Sātyaki) was being killed, the enraged son of Rukmiṇī (Pradyumna) then rushed into the midst, intending to liberate Śini's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 32. But while Śaineya (Sātyaki) was being killed, the enraged son of Rukmiṇī (Pradyumna) then rushed into the midst, intending to liberate Śini's son.

sanskrit: स भोजैः सह संयुक्तः सात्यकिश्चान्धकैः सह ।बहुत्वान्निहतौ तत्र उभौ कृष्णस्य पश्यतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa bhojaiḥ saha saṁyuktaḥ sātyakiścāndhakaiḥ saha ,bahutvānnihatau tatra ubhau kṛṣṇasya paśyataḥ.
english: He (Pradyumna) allied with the Bhojas, and Sātyaki with the Andhakas. Due to the overwhelming numbers (of their attackers), both were killed there while Kṛṣṇa watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 33. He (Pradyumna) allied with the Bhojas, and Sātyaki with the Andhakas. Due to the overwhelming numbers (of their attackers), both were killed there while Kṛṣṇa watched.

sanskrit: हतं दृष्ट्वा तु शैनेयं पुत्रं च यदुनन्दनः ।एरकाणां तदा मुष्टिं कोपाज्जग्राह केशवः ॥३४॥
transliteration: hataṁ dṛṣṭvā tu śaineyaṁ putraṁ ca yadunandanaḥ ,erakāṇāṁ tadā muṣṭiṁ kopājjagrāha keśavaḥ.
english: Having seen Śaineya (Sātyaki) and his own son (Pradyumna) killed, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), the delight of the Yadus, then, out of anger, grabbed a handful of "eraka" grasses.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 34. Having seen Śaineya (Sātyaki) and his own son (Pradyumna) killed, Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), the delight of the Yadus, then, out of anger, grabbed a handful of "eraka" grasses.

sanskrit: तदभून्मुसलं घोरं वज्रकल्पमयोमयम् ।जघान तेन कृष्णस्तान्येऽस्य प्रमुखतोऽभवन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tadabhūnmusalaṁ ghoraṁ vajrakalpamayomayam ,jaghāna tena kṛṣṇastānye'sya pramukhato'bhavan.
english: That (iron piece) became a terrible mace, resembling a thunderbolt and made of iron. Kṛṣṇa struck with it those who appeared before him.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 35. That (iron piece) became a terrible mace, resembling a thunderbolt and made of iron. Kṛṣṇa struck with it those who appeared before him.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्धकाश्च भोजाश्च शैनेया वृष्णयस्तथा ।जघ्नुरन्योन्यमाक्रन्दे मुसलैः कालचोदिताः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tato'ndhakāśca bhojāśca śaineyā vṛṣṇayastathā ,jaghnuranyonyamākrande musalaiḥ kālacoditāḥ.
english: Then, the Andhakas, Bhojas, Śaineyas, and Vṛṣṇis, impelled by time (kāla), struck each other with maces amidst the clamor.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 36. Then, the Andhakas, Bhojas, Śaineyas, and Vṛṣṇis, impelled by time (kāla), struck each other with maces amidst the clamor.

sanskrit: यस्तेषामेरकां कश्चिज्जग्राह रुषितो नृप ।वज्रभूतेव सा राजन्नदृश्यत तदा विभो ॥३७॥
transliteration: yasteṣāmerakāṁ kaścijjagrāha ruṣito nṛpa ,vajrabhūteva sā rājannadṛśyata tadā vibho.
english: O king (nṛpa), whoever among them angrily picked up an erakā grass blade, that very blade, O powerful one (vibhu), was then indeed seen as a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 37. O king (nṛpa), whoever among them angrily picked up an erakā grass blade, that very blade, O powerful one (vibhu), was then indeed seen as a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: तृणं च मुसलीभूतमपि तत्र व्यदृश्यत ।ब्रह्मदण्डकृतं सर्वमिति तद्विद्धि पार्थिव ॥३८॥
transliteration: tṛṇaṁ ca musalībhūtamapi tatra vyadṛśyata ,brahmadaṇḍakṛtaṁ sarvamiti tadviddhi pārthiva.
english: And even grass (tṛṇa) was seen there, having turned into a mace (musala). Know this, O earthly ruler (pārthiva), that all of this was brought about by the curse of the brahmins (brahmadaṇḍa).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 38. And even grass (tṛṇa) was seen there, having turned into a mace (musala). Know this, O earthly ruler (pārthiva), that all of this was brought about by the curse of the brahmins (brahmadaṇḍa).

sanskrit: आविध्याविध्य ते राजन्प्रक्षिपन्ति स्म यत्तृणम् ।तद्वज्रभूतं मुसलं व्यदृश्यत तदा दृढम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: āvidhyāvidhya te rājanprakṣipanti sma yattṛṇam ,tadvajrabhūtaṁ musalaṁ vyadṛśyata tadā dṛḍham.
english: O king, as they repeatedly struck each other, they threw what appeared to be merely a blade of grass. However, at that very moment, it was seen as a strong mace, transformed into a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 39. O king, as they repeatedly struck each other, they threw what appeared to be merely a blade of grass. However, at that very moment, it was seen as a strong mace, transformed into a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: अवधीत्पितरं पुत्रः पिता पुत्रं च भारत ।मत्ताः परिपतन्ति स्म पोथयन्तः परस्परम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: avadhītpitaraṁ putraḥ pitā putraṁ ca bhārata ,mattāḥ paripatanti sma pothayantaḥ parasparam.
english: O Bhārata, a son killed his father, and a father killed his son. Intoxicated, they roamed about, violently striking one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 40. O Bhārata, a son killed his father, and a father killed his son. Intoxicated, they roamed about, violently striking one another.

sanskrit: पतंगा इव चाग्नौ ते न्यपतन्कुकुरान्धकाः ।नासीत्पलायने बुद्धिर्वध्यमानस्य कस्यचित् ॥४१॥
transliteration: pataṁgā iva cāgnau te nyapatankukurāndhakāḥ ,nāsītpalāyane buddhirvadhyamānasya kasyacit.
english: And like moths into a fire, those Kukura and Andhaka clansmen fell. No one among those being slain had any thought of escape.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 41. And like moths into a fire, those Kukura and Andhaka clansmen fell. No one among those being slain had any thought of escape.

sanskrit: तं तु पश्यन्महाबाहुर्जानन्कालस्य पर्ययम् ।मुसलं समवष्टभ्य तस्थौ स मधुसूदनः ॥४२॥
transliteration: taṁ tu paśyanmahābāhurjānankālasya paryayam ,musalaṁ samavaṣṭabhya tasthau sa madhusūdanaḥ.
english: But the mighty-armed one, Madhusūdana, observing that and knowing the inevitable course of time, stood firm, grasping the mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 42. But the mighty-armed one, Madhusūdana, observing that and knowing the inevitable course of time, stood firm, grasping the mace.

sanskrit: साम्बं च निहतं दृष्ट्वा चारुदेष्णं च माधवः ।प्रद्युम्नं चानिरुद्धं च ततश्चुक्रोध भारत ॥४३॥
transliteration: sāmbaṁ ca nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā cārudeṣṇaṁ ca mādhavaḥ ,pradyumnaṁ cāniruddhaṁ ca tataścukrodha bhārata.
english: Having seen Samba, Carudeṣṇa, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha slain, Madhava (Kṛṣṇa) then became enraged, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 43. Having seen Samba, Carudeṣṇa, Pradyumna, and Aniruddha slain, Madhava (Kṛṣṇa) then became enraged, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: गदं वीक्ष्य शयानं च भृशं कोपसमन्वितः ।स निःशेषं तदा चक्रे शार्ङ्गचक्रगदाधरः ॥४४॥
transliteration: gadaṁ vīkṣya śayānaṁ ca bhṛśaṁ kopasamanvitaḥ ,sa niḥśeṣaṁ tadā cakre śārṅgacakragadādharaḥ.
english: Seeing Gada also lying slain, and intensely filled with rage, Kṛṣṇa, the wielder of the Śārṅga bow, discus, and mace, then brought about a complete annihilation.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 44. Seeing Gada also lying slain, and intensely filled with rage, Kṛṣṇa, the wielder of the Śārṅga bow, discus, and mace, then brought about a complete annihilation.

sanskrit: तं निघ्नन्तं महातेजा बभ्रुः परपुरंजयः ।दारुकश्चैव दाशार्हमूचतुर्यन्निबोध तत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: taṁ nighnantaṁ mahātejā babhruḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ ,dārukaścaiva dāśārhamūcaturyannibodha tat.
english: While Kṛṣṇa was thus slaying, the greatly glorious Babhru, conqueror of enemy cities, and Dāruka, both spoke to the descendant of Daśārha (Kṛṣṇa). Listen to what they said.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 45. While Kṛṣṇa was thus slaying, the greatly glorious Babhru, conqueror of enemy cities, and Dāruka, both spoke to the descendant of Daśārha (Kṛṣṇa). Listen to what they said.

sanskrit: भगवन्संहृतं सर्वं त्वया भूयिष्ठमच्युत ।रामस्य पदमन्विच्छ तत्र गच्छाम यत्र सः ॥४६॥
transliteration: bhagavansaṁhṛtaṁ sarvaṁ tvayā bhūyiṣṭhamacyuta ,rāmasya padamanviccha tatra gacchāma yatra saḥ.
english: "O Lord (Bhagavan), O Infallible One (Acyuta), most of everything has been destroyed by you. Now seek the path of Rama; let us go to where he is."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 4 Verse 46. "O Lord (Bhagavan), O Infallible One (Acyuta), most of everything has been destroyed by you. Now seek the path of Rama; let us go to where he is."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो ययुर्दारुकः केशवश्च बभ्रुश्च रामस्य पदं पतन्तः ।अथापश्यन्राममनन्तवीर्यं वृक्षे स्थितं चिन्तयानं विविक्ते ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato yayurdārukaḥ keśavaśca; babhruśca rāmasya padaṁ patantaḥ ,athāpaśyanrāmamanantavīryaṁ; vṛkṣe sthitaṁ cintayānaṁ vivikte.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Dāruka, Keśava (Krishna), and Babhru followed Rāma's path. There, they saw Rāma, of infinite valor, seated under a tree, contemplating in a secluded spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Dāruka, Keśava (Krishna), and Babhru followed Rāma's path. There, they saw Rāma, of infinite valor, seated under a tree, contemplating in a secluded spot.

sanskrit: ततः समासाद्य महानुभावः कृष्णस्तदा दारुकमन्वशासत् ।गत्वा कुरूञ्शीघ्रमिमं महान्तं पार्थाय शंसस्व वधं यदूनाम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ samāsādya mahānubhāvaḥ; kṛṣṇastadā dārukamanvaśāsat ,gatvā kurūñśīghramimaṁ mahāntaṁ; pārthāya śaṁsasva vadhaṁ yadūnām.
english: Then, having approached, the highly glorious Krishna then instructed Dāruka: "Go quickly to the Kurus and report this immense destruction of the Yadus to Arjuna."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Then, having approached, the highly glorious Krishna then instructed Dāruka: "Go quickly to the Kurus and report this immense destruction of the Yadus to Arjuna."

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनः क्षिप्रमिहोपयातु श्रुत्वा मृतान्यादवान्ब्रह्मशापात् ।इत्येवमुक्तः स ययौ रथेन कुरूंस्तदा दारुको नष्टचेताः ॥३॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaḥ kṣipramihopayātu; śrutvā mṛtānyādavānbrahmaśāpāt ,ityevamuktaḥ sa yayau rathena; kurūṁstadā dāruko naṣṭacetāḥ.
english: "Let Arjuna come here quickly upon hearing of the Yadus' demise due to the brahmin's curse." Having been thus instructed, Dāruka, with a distraught mind, then departed by chariot for the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. "Let Arjuna come here quickly upon hearing of the Yadus' demise due to the brahmin's curse." Having been thus instructed, Dāruka, with a distraught mind, then departed by chariot for the Kurus.

sanskrit: ततो गते दारुके केशवोऽथ दृष्ट्वान्तिके बभ्रुमुवाच वाक्यम् ।स्त्रियो भवान्रक्षतु यातु शीघ्रं नैता हिंस्युर्दस्यवो वित्तलोभात् ॥४॥
transliteration: tato gate dāruke keśavo'tha; dṛṣṭvāntike babhrumuvāca vākyam ,striyo bhavānrakṣatu yātu śīghraṁ; naitā hiṁsyurdasyavo vittalobhāt.
english: Then, after Dāruka had left, Keśava (Krishna), seeing Babhru nearby, spoke these words: "You must protect the women; go quickly. Robbers might otherwise harm them out of greed for riches."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. Then, after Dāruka had left, Keśava (Krishna), seeing Babhru nearby, spoke these words: "You must protect the women; go quickly. Robbers might otherwise harm them out of greed for riches."

sanskrit: स प्रस्थितः केशवेनानुशिष्टो मदातुरो ज्ञातिवधार्दितश्च ।तं वै यान्तं संनिधौ केशवस्य त्वरन्तमेकं सहसैव बभ्रुम् ।ब्रह्मानुशप्तमवधीन्महद्वै कूटोन्मुक्तं मुसलं लुब्धकस्य ॥५॥
transliteration: sa prasthitaḥ keśavenānuśiṣṭo; madāturo jñātivadhārditaśca ,taṁ vai yāntaṁ saṁnidhau keśavasya; tvarantamekaṁ sahasaiva babhrum ,brahmānuśaptamavadhīnmahadvai; kūṭonmuktaṁ musalaṁ lubdhakasya.
english: Having departed as instructed by Keśava, he (Balarāma) was afflicted by intoxication and distressed by the slaughter of his kinsmen. Indeed, while Babhru, who had been cursed by a brahmin, was hurrying alone in Keśava's presence, a great club of a hunter, released from its snare, suddenly killed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. Having departed as instructed by Keśava, he (Balarāma) was afflicted by intoxication and distressed by the slaughter of his kinsmen. Indeed, while Babhru, who had been cursed by a brahmin, was hurrying alone in Keśava's presence, a great club of a hunter, released from its snare, suddenly killed him.

sanskrit: ततो दृष्ट्वा निहतं बभ्रुमाह कृष्णो वाक्यं भ्रातरमग्रजं तु ।इहैव त्वं मां प्रतीक्षस्व राम यावत्स्त्रियो ज्ञातिवशाः करोमि ॥६॥
transliteration: tato dṛṣṭvā nihataṁ babhrumāha; kṛṣṇo vākyaṁ bhrātaramagrajaṁ tu ,ihaiva tvaṁ māṁ pratīkṣasva rāma; yāvatstriyo jñātivaśāḥ karomi.
english: Then, having seen Babhru slain, Kṛṣṇa spoke this statement to his elder brother: "Rāma, wait for me right here until I secure the women under the care of their kinsmen."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. Then, having seen Babhru slain, Kṛṣṇa spoke this statement to his elder brother: "Rāma, wait for me right here until I secure the women under the care of their kinsmen."

sanskrit: ततः पुरीं द्वारवतीं प्रविश्य जनार्दनः पितरं प्राह वाक्यम् ।स्त्रियो भवान्रक्षतु नः समग्रा धनंजयस्यागमनं प्रतीक्षन् ।रामो वनान्ते प्रतिपालयन्मामास्तेऽद्याहं तेन समागमिष्ये ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ purīṁ dvāravatīṁ praviśya; janārdanaḥ pitaraṁ prāha vākyam ,striyo bhavānrakṣatu naḥ samagrā; dhanaṁjayasyāgamanaṁ pratīkṣan ,rāmo vanānte pratipālayanmā;māste'dyāhaṁ tena samāgamiṣye.
english: Then, having entered the city of Dvārakā, Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) spoke to his father: "You should protect all our women while awaiting Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) arrival. Rāma (Balarāma) is waiting for me at the edge of the forest, and today I will go and join him."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Then, having entered the city of Dvārakā, Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa) spoke to his father: "You should protect all our women while awaiting Dhananjaya's (Arjuna's) arrival. Rāma (Balarāma) is waiting for me at the edge of the forest, and today I will go and join him."

sanskrit: दृष्टं मयेदं निधनं यदूनां राज्ञां च पूर्वं कुरुपुंगवानाम् ।नाहं विना यदुभिर्यादवानां पुरीमिमां द्रष्टुमिहाद्य शक्तः ॥८॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭaṁ mayedaṁ nidhanaṁ yadūnāṁ; rājñāṁ ca pūrvaṁ kurupuṁgavānām ,nāhaṁ vinā yadubhiryādavānāṁ; purīmimāṁ draṣṭumihādya śaktaḥ.
english: I have witnessed this destruction of the Yādavas, just as I previously saw that of the kings, the foremost among the Kurus. Without the Yādavas, I am no longer able to behold this city of the Yādavas here today.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. I have witnessed this destruction of the Yādavas, just as I previously saw that of the kings, the foremost among the Kurus. Without the Yādavas, I am no longer able to behold this city of the Yādavas here today.

sanskrit: तपश्चरिष्यामि निबोध तन्मे रामेण सार्धं वनमभ्युपेत्य ।इतीदमुक्त्वा शिरसास्य पादौ संस्पृश्य कृष्णस्त्वरितो जगाम ॥९॥
transliteration: tapaścariṣyāmi nibodha tanme; rāmeṇa sārdhaṁ vanamabhyupetya ,itīdamuktvā śirasāsya pādau; saṁspṛśya kṛṣṇastvarito jagāma.
english: I will undertake spiritual austerities (tapas). Please understand this from me. Having gone to the forest with Rama, Krishna, after saying this and touching his (Rama's) feet with his head, quickly departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. I will undertake spiritual austerities (tapas). Please understand this from me. Having gone to the forest with Rama, Krishna, after saying this and touching his (Rama's) feet with his head, quickly departed.

sanskrit: ततो महान्निनदः प्रादुरासीत्सस्त्रीकुमारस्य पुरस्य तस्य ।अथाब्रवीत्केशवः संनिवर्त्य शब्दं श्रुत्वा योषितां क्रोशतीनाम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato mahānninadaḥ prādurāsī;tsastrīkumārasya purasya tasya ,athābravītkeśavaḥ saṁnivartya; śabdaṁ śrutvā yoṣitāṁ krośatīnām.
english: Then a great clamor arose from that city, encompassing its women and children. Then, turning back after hearing the sound of the wailing women, Keśava spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. Then a great clamor arose from that city, encompassing its women and children. Then, turning back after hearing the sound of the wailing women, Keśava spoke.

sanskrit: पुरीमिमामेष्यति सव्यसाची स वो दुःखान्मोचयिता नराग्र्यः ।ततो गत्वा केशवस्तं ददर्श रामं वने स्थितमेकं विविक्ते ॥११॥
transliteration: purīmimāmeṣyati savyasācī; sa vo duḥkhānmocayitā narāgryaḥ ,tato gatvā keśavastaṁ dadarśa; rāmaṁ vane sthitamekaṁ vivikte.
english: Savyasācī will come to this city. He, that foremost among men, will liberate you from sorrow. Then, having proceeded further, Keśava saw Rama, dwelling alone in a secluded spot in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. Savyasācī will come to this city. He, that foremost among men, will liberate you from sorrow. Then, having proceeded further, Keśava saw Rama, dwelling alone in a secluded spot in the forest.

sanskrit: अथापश्यद्योगयुक्तस्य तस्य नागं मुखान्निःसरन्तं महान्तम् ।श्वेतं ययौ स ततः प्रेक्ष्यमाणो महार्णवो येन महानुभावः ॥१२॥
transliteration: athāpaśyadyogayuktasya tasya; nāgaṁ mukhānniḥsarantaṁ mahāntam ,śvetaṁ yayau sa tataḥ prekṣyamāṇo; mahārṇavo yena mahānubhāvaḥ.
english: Then he (Keśava) saw a great, white serpent (nāga) emerging from the mouth of that one (Rama) who was engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga). The great-souled Keśava, (still) being watched, then went in the direction of the great ocean, following the path of the (serpent).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. Then he (Keśava) saw a great, white serpent (nāga) emerging from the mouth of that one (Rama) who was engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga). The great-souled Keśava, (still) being watched, then went in the direction of the great ocean, following the path of the (serpent).

sanskrit: सहस्रशीर्षः पर्वताभोगवर्ष्मा रक्ताननः स्वां तनुं तां विमुच्य ।सम्यक्च तं सागरः प्रत्यगृह्णान्नागा दिव्याः सरितश्चैव पुण्याः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sahasraśīrṣaḥ parvatābhogavarṣmā; raktānanaḥ svāṁ tanuṁ tāṁ vimucya ,samyakca taṁ sāgaraḥ pratyagṛhṇā;nnāgā divyāḥ saritaścaiva puṇyāḥ.
english: The thousand-headed one, whose body was as vast as a mountain and whose face was red, having abandoned his own body, was properly received by the ocean. Divine serpents (nāgas) and sacred rivers, indeed holy ones, were also present.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. The thousand-headed one, whose body was as vast as a mountain and whose face was red, having abandoned his own body, was properly received by the ocean. Divine serpents (nāgas) and sacred rivers, indeed holy ones, were also present.

sanskrit: कर्कोटको वासुकिस्तक्षकश्च पृथुश्रवा वरुणः कुञ्जरश्च ।मिश्री शङ्खः कुमुदः पुण्डरीकस्तथा नागो धृतराष्ट्रो महात्मा ॥१४॥
transliteration: karkoṭako vāsukistakṣakaśca; pṛthuśravā varuṇaḥ kuñjaraśca ,miśrī śaṅkhaḥ kumudaḥ puṇḍarīka;stathā nāgo dhṛtarāṣṭro mahātmā.
english: Karkoṭaka, Vāsuki, and Takṣaka; Pṛthuśravā, Varuṇa, and Kuñjara; Miśrī, Śaṅkha, Kumuda, Puṇḍarīka, and also the great-souled nāga Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. Karkoṭaka, Vāsuki, and Takṣaka; Pṛthuśravā, Varuṇa, and Kuñjara; Miśrī, Śaṅkha, Kumuda, Puṇḍarīka, and also the great-souled nāga Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: ह्रादः क्राथः शितिकण्ठोऽग्रतेजास्तथा नागौ चक्रमन्दातिषण्डौ ।नागश्रेष्ठो दुर्मुखश्चाम्बरीषः स्वयं राजा वरुणश्चापि राजन् ।प्रत्युद्गम्य स्वागतेनाभ्यनन्दंस्तेऽपूजयंश्चार्घ्यपाद्यक्रियाभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: hrādaḥ krāthaḥ śitikaṇṭho'gratejā;stathā nāgau cakramandātiṣaṇḍau ,nāgaśreṣṭho durmukhaścāmbarīṣaḥ; svayaṁ rājā varuṇaścāpi rājan ,pratyudgamya svāgatenābhyanandaṁ;ste'pūjayaṁścārghyapādyakriyābhiḥ.
english: Hrada, Krātha, Śitikaṇṭha, and Agratejas; likewise the two nāgas Cakramaṇḍa and Atiṣaṇḍa; the foremost nāga Durmukha, and Ambarīṣa; and King Varuṇa himself, O king! Having gone forth to meet him, they welcomed him with a greeting and worshipped him with offerings of water (arghya) and foot-washings (pādya).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. Hrada, Krātha, Śitikaṇṭha, and Agratejas; likewise the two nāgas Cakramaṇḍa and Atiṣaṇḍa; the foremost nāga Durmukha, and Ambarīṣa; and King Varuṇa himself, O king! Having gone forth to meet him, they welcomed him with a greeting and worshipped him with offerings of water (arghya) and foot-washings (pādya).

sanskrit: ततो गते भ्रातरि वासुदेवो जानन्सर्वा गतयो दिव्यदृष्टिः ।वने शून्ये विचरंश्चिन्तयानो भूमौ ततः संविवेशाग्र्यतेजाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato gate bhrātari vāsudevo; jānansarvā gatayo divyadṛṣṭiḥ ,vane śūnye vicaraṁścintayāno; bhūmau tataḥ saṁviveśāgryatejāḥ.
english: Then, after his brother had departed, Vāsudeva, who knew all destinies and possessed divine vision, wandered in the desolate forest, contemplating deeply. Then, the glorious one lay down on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. Then, after his brother had departed, Vāsudeva, who knew all destinies and possessed divine vision, wandered in the desolate forest, contemplating deeply. Then, the glorious one lay down on the ground.

sanskrit: सर्वं हि तेन प्राक्तदा वित्तमासीद्गान्धार्या यद्वाक्यमुक्तः स पूर्वम् ।दुर्वाससा पायसोच्छिष्टलिप्ते यच्चाप्युक्तं तच्च सस्मार कृष्णः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sarvaṁ hi tena prāktadā vittamāsī;dgāndhāryā yadvākyamuktaḥ sa pūrvam ,durvāsasā pāyasocchiṣṭalipte; yaccāpyuktaṁ tacca sasmāra kṛṣṇaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa indeed knew all this beforehand. He remembered the words spoken to him previously by Gāndhārī, and also what Durvāsā had uttered when he (Durvāsā) was smeared with the remnants of rice pudding.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. Kṛṣṇa indeed knew all this beforehand. He remembered the words spoken to him previously by Gāndhārī, and also what Durvāsā had uttered when he (Durvāsā) was smeared with the remnants of rice pudding.

sanskrit: स चिन्तयानोऽन्धकवृष्णिनाशं कुरुक्षयं चैव महानुभावः ।मेने ततः संक्रमणस्य कालं ततश्चकारेन्द्रियसंनिरोधम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa cintayāno'ndhakavṛṣṇināśaṁ; kurukṣayaṁ caiva mahānubhāvaḥ ,mene tataḥ saṁkramaṇasya kālaṁ; tataścakārendriyasaṁnirodham.
english: Reflecting on the annihilation of the Andhaka and Vṛṣṇi clans, as well as the destruction of the Kurus, that greatly powerful one (Kṛṣṇa) then deemed it the time for his departure. Consequently, he began to restrain his senses.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. Reflecting on the annihilation of the Andhaka and Vṛṣṇi clans, as well as the destruction of the Kurus, that greatly powerful one (Kṛṣṇa) then deemed it the time for his departure. Consequently, he began to restrain his senses.

sanskrit: स संनिरुद्धेन्द्रियवाङ्मनास्तु शिश्ये महायोगमुपेत्य कृष्णः ।जराथ तं देशमुपाजगाम लुब्धस्तदानीं मृगलिप्सुरुग्रः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa saṁniruddhendriyavāṅmanāstu; śiśye mahāyogamupetya kṛṣṇaḥ ,jarātha taṁ deśamupājagāma; lubdhastadānīṁ mṛgalipsurugraḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa, with his senses, speech, and mind completely restrained, lay down, having entered into a state of profound meditation (yoga). Then, the fierce hunter Jarā, who was at that time seeking game, came to that very spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. Kṛṣṇa, with his senses, speech, and mind completely restrained, lay down, having entered into a state of profound meditation (yoga). Then, the fierce hunter Jarā, who was at that time seeking game, came to that very spot.

sanskrit: स केशवं योगयुक्तं शयानं मृगाशङ्की लुब्धकः सायकेन ।जराविध्यत्पादतले त्वरावांस्तं चाभितस्तज्जिघृक्षुर्जगाम ।अथापश्यत्पुरुषं योगयुक्तं पीताम्बरं लुब्धकोऽनेकबाहुम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa keśavaṁ yogayuktaṁ śayānaṁ; mṛgāśaṅkī lubdhakaḥ sāyakena ,jarāvidhyatpādatale tvarāvāṁ;staṁ cābhitastajjighṛkṣurjagāma ,athāpaśyatpuruṣaṁ yogayuktaṁ; pītāmbaraṁ lubdhako'nekabāhum.
english: Mistaking Keśava, who lay absorbed in (yoga), for a deer, the hunter Jarā swiftly struck him in the sole of the foot with an arrow. Hastily, desiring to seize it, he approached Kṛṣṇa. Then, the hunter saw a man engaged in (yoga), wearing yellow garments, and having many arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. Mistaking Keśava, who lay absorbed in (yoga), for a deer, the hunter Jarā swiftly struck him in the sole of the foot with an arrow. Hastily, desiring to seize it, he approached Kṛṣṇa. Then, the hunter saw a man engaged in (yoga), wearing yellow garments, and having many arms.

sanskrit: मत्वात्मानमपराद्धं स तस्य जग्राह पादौ शिरसा चार्तरूपः ।आश्वासयत्तं महात्मा तदानीं गच्छन्नूर्ध्वं रोदसी व्याप्य लक्ष्म्या ॥२१॥
transliteration: matvātmānamaparāddhaṁ sa tasya; jagrāha pādau śirasā cārtarūpaḥ ,āśvāsayattaṁ mahātmā tadānīṁ; gacchannūrdhvaṁ rodasī vyāpya lakṣmyā.
english: Thinking himself to have offended, he, in distress, clasped his feet with his head. The great-souled one (mahātmā) then comforted him as he ascended, pervading heaven and earth with his glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. Thinking himself to have offended, he, in distress, clasped his feet with his head. The great-souled one (mahātmā) then comforted him as he ascended, pervading heaven and earth with his glory.

sanskrit: दिवं प्राप्तं वासवोऽथाश्विनौ च रुद्रादित्या वसवश्चाथ विश्वे ।प्रत्युद्ययुर्मुनयश्चापि सिद्धा गन्धर्वमुख्याश्च सहाप्सरोभिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: divaṁ prāptaṁ vāsavo'thāśvinau ca; rudrādityā vasavaścātha viśve ,pratyudyayurmunayaścāpi siddhā; gandharvamukhyāśca sahāpsarobhiḥ.
english: When he (Krishna) reached heaven, Indra, the two Aśvins, the Rudras, Ādityas, Vasus, and all the Viśvedevas came forth to meet him. So too did the sages (muni) and Siddhas, along with the chief Gandharvas and Apsaras.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. When he (Krishna) reached heaven, Indra, the two Aśvins, the Rudras, Ādityas, Vasus, and all the Viśvedevas came forth to meet him. So too did the sages (muni) and Siddhas, along with the chief Gandharvas and Apsaras.

sanskrit: ततो राजन्भगवानुग्रतेजा नारायणः प्रभवश्चाव्ययश्च ।योगाचार्यो रोदसी व्याप्य लक्ष्म्या स्थानं प्राप स्वं महात्माप्रमेयम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato rājanbhagavānugratejā; nārāyaṇaḥ prabhavaścāvyayaśca ,yogācāryo rodasī vyāpya lakṣmyā; sthānaṁ prāpa svaṁ mahātmāprameyam.
english: Then, O king, the divine Nārāyaṇa, of fierce energy, the origin and the imperishable, the great teacher of (yoga), that great-souled one (mahātmā), pervading heaven and earth with his glory, reached his own immeasurable abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. Then, O king, the divine Nārāyaṇa, of fierce energy, the origin and the imperishable, the great teacher of (yoga), that great-souled one (mahātmā), pervading heaven and earth with his glory, reached his own immeasurable abode.

sanskrit: ततो देवैरृषिभिश्चापि कृष्णः समागतश्चारणैश्चैव राजन् ।गन्धर्वाग्र्यैरप्सरोभिर्वराभिः सिद्धैः साध्यैश्चानतैः पूज्यमानः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato devairṛṣibhiścāpi kṛṣṇaḥ; samāgataścāraṇaiścaiva rājan ,gandharvāgryairapsarobhirvarābhiḥ; siddhaiḥ sādhyaiścānataiḥ pūjyamānaḥ.
english: Then, O king, Krishna, being met by the gods, sages, and Cāraṇas, and being worshipped by the chief Gandharvas, excellent Apsaras, Siddhas, and Sādhyas, who all bowed down to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. Then, O king, Krishna, being met by the gods, sages, and Cāraṇas, and being worshipped by the chief Gandharvas, excellent Apsaras, Siddhas, and Sādhyas, who all bowed down to him.

sanskrit: ते वै देवाः प्रत्यनन्दन्त राजन्मुनिश्रेष्ठा वाग्भिरानर्चुरीशम् ।गन्धर्वाश्चाप्युपतस्थुः स्तुवन्तः प्रीत्या चैनं पुरुहूतोऽभ्यनन्दत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: te vai devāḥ pratyanandanta rāja;nmuniśreṣṭhā vāgbhirānarcurīśam ,gandharvāścāpyupatasthuḥ stuvantaḥ; prītyā cainaṁ puruhūto'bhyanandat.
english: O King, indeed those gods rejoiced. The foremost among the sages honored the Lord with their words. The Gandharvas also stood by, praising, and Puruhūta (Indra) greeted him with affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. O King, indeed those gods rejoiced. The foremost among the sages honored the Lord with their words. The Gandharvas also stood by, praising, and Puruhūta (Indra) greeted him with affection.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।दारुकोऽपि कुरून्गत्वा दृष्ट्वा पार्थान्महारथान् ।आचष्ट मौसले वृष्णीनन्योन्येनोपसंहृतान् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dāruko'pi kurūngatvā dṛṣṭvā pārthānmahārathān ,ācaṣṭa mausale vṛṣṇīnanyonyenopasaṁhṛtān.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Dāruka, having gone to the land of the Kurus and seen the Pārthas, the great warriors, reported that the Vṛṣṇis had been mutually annihilated in the mausala (club-battle).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Dāruka, having gone to the land of the Kurus and seen the Pārthas, the great warriors, reported that the Vṛṣṇis had been mutually annihilated in the mausala (club-battle).

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा विनष्टान्वार्ष्णेयान्सभोजकुकुरान्धकान् ।पाण्डवाः शोकसंतप्ता वित्रस्तमनसोऽभवन् ॥२॥
transliteration: śrutvā vinaṣṭānvārṣṇeyānsabhojakukurāndhakān ,pāṇḍavāḥ śokasaṁtaptā vitrastamanaso'bhavan.
english: Hearing that the Vārṣṇeyas, along with the Bhojas, Kukuras, and Andhakas, had perished, the Pāṇḍavas became consumed by sorrow and greatly disturbed in mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. Hearing that the Vārṣṇeyas, along with the Bhojas, Kukuras, and Andhakas, had perished, the Pāṇḍavas became consumed by sorrow and greatly disturbed in mind.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनस्तानामन्त्र्य केशवस्य प्रियः सखा ।प्रययौ मातुलं द्रष्टुं नेदमस्तीति चाब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: tato'rjunastānāmantrya keśavasya priyaḥ sakhā ,prayayau mātulaṁ draṣṭuṁ nedamastīti cābravīt.
english: Then Arjuna, dear friend of Keśava (Krishna), having addressed the Pāṇḍavas, went to see his maternal uncle (Vasudeva) and declared, 'This (world/situation) is no longer as it was.'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. Then Arjuna, dear friend of Keśava (Krishna), having addressed the Pāṇḍavas, went to see his maternal uncle (Vasudeva) and declared, 'This (world/situation) is no longer as it was.'

sanskrit: स वृष्णिनिलयं गत्वा दारुकेण सह प्रभो ।ददर्श द्वारकां वीरो मृतनाथामिव स्त्रियम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa vṛṣṇinilayaṁ gatvā dārukeṇa saha prabho ,dadarśa dvārakāṁ vīro mṛtanāthāmiva striyam.
english: O Lord, the hero (Arjuna), having arrived at the abode of the Vṛṣṇis with Dāruka, saw Dvārakā resembling a woman whose husband was deceased.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. O Lord, the hero (Arjuna), having arrived at the abode of the Vṛṣṇis with Dāruka, saw Dvārakā resembling a woman whose husband was deceased.

sanskrit: याः स्म ता लोकनाथेन नाथवत्यः पुराभवन् ।तास्त्वनाथास्तदा नाथं पार्थं दृष्ट्वा विचुक्रुशुः ॥५॥
transliteration: yāḥ sma tā lokanāthena nāthavatyaḥ purābhavan ,tāstvanāthāstadā nāthaṁ pārthaṁ dṛṣṭvā vicukruśuḥ.
english: Those women who were formerly protected by the Lord of the world (Krishna), and thus had a master, now, being without a master (anāthāḥ), cried out loudly upon seeing Pārtha (Arjuna) as their master.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. Those women who were formerly protected by the Lord of the world (Krishna), and thus had a master, now, being without a master (anāthāḥ), cried out loudly upon seeing Pārtha (Arjuna) as their master.

sanskrit: षोडशस्त्रीसहस्राणि वासुदेवपरिग्रहः ।तासामासीन्महान्नादो दृष्ट्वैवार्जुनमागतम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ṣoḍaśastrīsahasrāṇi vāsudevaparigrahaḥ ,tāsāmāsīnmahānnādo dṛṣṭvaivārjunamāgatam.
english: Sixteen thousand women, the consorts of Vāsudeva (Krishna), let out a great outcry simply upon seeing Arjuna arrive.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. Sixteen thousand women, the consorts of Vāsudeva (Krishna), let out a great outcry simply upon seeing Arjuna arrive.

sanskrit: तास्तु दृष्ट्वैव कौरव्यो बाष्पेण पिहितोऽर्जुनः ।हीनाः कृष्णेन पुत्रैश्च नाशकत्सोऽभिवीक्षितुम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tāstu dṛṣṭvaiva kauravyo bāṣpeṇa pihito'rjunaḥ ,hīnāḥ kṛṣṇena putraiśca nāśakatso'bhivīkṣitum.
english: But Arjuna, the descendant of Kuru (kauravya), upon seeing those women, was overcome by tears. Since they were deprived of Krishna and their sons, he was unable to look at them directly.
