search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. But Arjuna, the descendant of Kuru (kauravya), upon seeing those women, was overcome by tears. Since they were deprived of Krishna and their sons, he was unable to look at them directly.

sanskrit: तां स वृष्ण्यन्धकजलां हयमीनां रथोडुपाम् ।वादित्ररथघोषौघां वेश्मतीर्थमहाग्रहाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tāṁ sa vṛṣṇyandhakajalāṁ hayamīnāṁ rathoḍupām ,vāditrarathaghoṣaughāṁ veśmatīrthamahāgrahām.
english: That (city), which had the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas as its water, horses as its fish, and chariots as its rafts; whose tumultuous currents were the sounds of musical instruments and chariot roars, and whose houses served as sacred bathing places (tīrtha) and contained great aquatic predators (mahāgraha).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. That (city), which had the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas as its water, horses as its fish, and chariots as its rafts; whose tumultuous currents were the sounds of musical instruments and chariot roars, and whose houses served as sacred bathing places (tīrtha) and contained great aquatic predators (mahāgraha).

sanskrit: रत्नशैवलसंघाटां वज्रप्राकारमालिनीम् ।रथ्यास्रोतोजलावर्तां चत्वरस्तिमितह्रदाम् ॥९॥
transliteration: ratnaśaivalasaṁghāṭāṁ vajraprākāramālinīm ,rathyāsrotojalāvartāṁ catvarastimitahradām.
english: Whose heaps of jewels were its algae, whose diamond walls formed its garlands (of waves or banks), whose street currents were its whirlpools, and whose squares were its still, deep pools.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. Whose heaps of jewels were its algae, whose diamond walls formed its garlands (of waves or banks), whose street currents were its whirlpools, and whose squares were its still, deep pools.

sanskrit: रामकृष्णमहाग्राहां द्वारकासरितं तदा ।कालपाशग्रहां घोरां नदीं वैतरणीमिव ॥१०॥
transliteration: rāmakṛṣṇamahāgrāhāṁ dvārakāsaritaṁ tadā ,kālapāśagrahāṁ ghorāṁ nadīṁ vaitaraṇīmiva.
english: Then, that Dvārakā-river, which had Rāma and Kṛṣṇa as its great aquatic creatures (mahāgraha), (now, after their departure) had the noose of time as its great aquatic creatures, dreadful like the river Vaitaraṇī.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. Then, that Dvārakā-river, which had Rāma and Kṛṣṇa as its great aquatic creatures (mahāgraha), (now, after their departure) had the noose of time as its great aquatic creatures, dreadful like the river Vaitaraṇī.

sanskrit: तां ददर्शार्जुनो धीमान्विहीनां वृष्णिपुंगवैः ।गतश्रियं निरानन्दां पद्मिनीं शिशिरे यथा ॥११॥
transliteration: tāṁ dadarśārjuno dhīmānvihīnāṁ vṛṣṇipuṁgavaiḥ ,gataśriyaṁ nirānandāṁ padminīṁ śiśire yathā.
english: The intelligent Arjuna saw that city, devoid of the foremost Vṛṣṇis, its glory gone, joyless, just like a lotus pond in winter.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. The intelligent Arjuna saw that city, devoid of the foremost Vṛṣṇis, its glory gone, joyless, just like a lotus pond in winter.

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा द्वारकां पार्थस्ताश्च कृष्णस्य योषितः ।सस्वनं बाष्पमुत्सृज्य निपपात महीतले ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā dvārakāṁ pārthastāśca kṛṣṇasya yoṣitaḥ ,sasvanaṁ bāṣpamutsṛjya nipapāta mahītale.
english: Having seen Dvārakā and Kṛṣṇa's wives, Arjuna (Pārtha) fell to the ground, loudly shedding tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. Having seen Dvārakā and Kṛṣṇa's wives, Arjuna (Pārtha) fell to the ground, loudly shedding tears.

sanskrit: सात्राजिती ततः सत्या रुक्मिणी च विशां पते ।अभिपत्य प्ररुरुदुः परिवार्य धनंजयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sātrājitī tataḥ satyā rukmiṇī ca viśāṁ pate ,abhipatya praruruduḥ parivārya dhanaṁjayam.
english: O lord of people, then Satyabhāmā and Rukmiṇī approached and surrounded Arjuna (Dhananjaya), and cried loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. O lord of people, then Satyabhāmā and Rukmiṇī approached and surrounded Arjuna (Dhananjaya), and cried loudly.

sanskrit: ततस्ताः काञ्चने पीठे समुत्थायोपवेश्य च ।अब्रुवन्त्यो महात्मानं परिवार्योपतस्थिरे ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastāḥ kāñcane pīṭhe samutthāyopaveśya ca ,abruvantyo mahātmānaṁ parivāryopatasthire.
english: Then, having risen and seated the great-souled Arjuna on a golden seat, those women surrounded him and stood silently before him.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. Then, having risen and seated the great-souled Arjuna on a golden seat, those women surrounded him and stood silently before him.

sanskrit: ततः संस्तूय गोविन्दं कथयित्वा च पाण्डवः ।आश्वास्य ताः स्त्रियश्चापि मातुलं द्रष्टुमभ्यगात् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁstūya govindaṁ kathayitvā ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,āśvāsya tāḥ striyaścāpi mātulaṁ draṣṭumabhyagāt.
english: Then Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), having first praised Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), then having spoken and also comforted those women, went to see his maternal uncle.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. Then Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), having first praised Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), then having spoken and also comforted those women, went to see his maternal uncle.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तं शयानं महात्मानं वीरमानकदुन्दुभिम् ।पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तं ददर्श कुरुपुंगवः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taṁ śayānaṁ mahātmānaṁ vīramānakadundubhim ,putraśokābhisaṁtaptaṁ dadarśa kurupuṁgavaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The best of the Kurus (Arjuna) saw that great-souled (mahātman) hero, Anakadundubhi (Vasudeva), lying down, afflicted by grief for his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: The best of the Kurus (Arjuna) saw that great-souled (mahātman) hero, Anakadundubhi (Vasudeva), lying down, afflicted by grief for his sons.

sanskrit: तस्याश्रुपरिपूर्णाक्षो व्यूढोरस्को महाभुजः ।आर्तस्यार्ततरः पार्थः पादौ जग्राह भारत ॥२॥
transliteration: tasyāśruparipūrṇākṣo vyūḍhorasko mahābhujaḥ ,ārtasyārtataraḥ pārthaḥ pādau jagrāha bhārata.
english: With eyes full of tears, broad-chested, and mighty-armed, Pārtha, more distressed than the already distressed Vasudeva, clasped his feet, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. With eyes full of tears, broad-chested, and mighty-armed, Pārtha, more distressed than the already distressed Vasudeva, clasped his feet, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: समालिङ्ग्यार्जुनं वृद्धः स भुजाभ्यां महाभुजः ।रुदन्पुत्रान्स्मरन्सर्वान्विललाप सुविह्वलः ।भ्रातॄन्पुत्रांश्च पौत्रांश्च दौहित्रांश्च सखीनपि ॥३॥
transliteration: samāliṅgyārjunaṁ vṛddhaḥ sa bhujābhyāṁ mahābhujaḥ ,rudanputrānsmaransarvānvilalāpa suvihvalaḥ ,bhrātṝnputrāṁśca pautrāṁśca dauhitrāṁśca sakhīnapi.
english: Embracing Arjuna with his two mighty arms, the aged (Vasudeva), greatly agitated, wept, remembering all his sons, brothers, grandsons, great-grandsons, and even friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. Embracing Arjuna with his two mighty arms, the aged (Vasudeva), greatly agitated, wept, remembering all his sons, brothers, grandsons, great-grandsons, and even friends.

sanskrit: वसुदेव उवाच ।यैर्जिता भूमिपालाश्च दैत्याश्च शतशोऽर्जुन ।तान्दृष्ट्वा नेह पश्यामि जीवाम्यर्जुन दुर्मरः ॥४॥
transliteration: vasudeva uvāca ,yairjitā bhūmipālāśca daityāśca śataśo'rjuna ,tāndṛṣṭvā neha paśyāmi jīvāmyarjuna durmaraḥ.
english: Vasudeva said: O Arjuna, having seen those by whom hundreds of kings and demons were conquered, now that I do not see them here, I (find it) difficult to die, Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. Vasudeva said: O Arjuna, having seen those by whom hundreds of kings and demons were conquered, now that I do not see them here, I (find it) difficult to die, Arjuna.

sanskrit: यौ तावर्जुन शिष्यौ ते प्रियौ बहुमतौ सदा ।तयोरपनयात्पार्थ वृष्णयो निधनं गताः ॥५॥
transliteration: yau tāvarjuna śiṣyau te priyau bahumatau sadā ,tayorapanayātpārtha vṛṣṇayo nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: O Pārtha, due to the transgression of those two disciples of yours, Arjuna, who were always dear and highly regarded, the Vṛṣṇis met their end.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. O Pārtha, due to the transgression of those two disciples of yours, Arjuna, who were always dear and highly regarded, the Vṛṣṇis met their end.

sanskrit: यौ तौ वृष्णिप्रवीराणां द्वावेवातिरथौ मतौ ।प्रद्युम्नो युयुधानश्च कथयन्कत्थसे च यौ ॥६॥
transliteration: yau tau vṛṣṇipravīrāṇāṁ dvāvevātirathau matau ,pradyumno yuyudhānaśca kathayankatthase ca yau.
english: Those two, Pradyumna and Yuyudhāna, who are considered the two foremost mahārathas among the Vṛṣṇi heroes, are the ones whom you speak of with praise.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. Those two, Pradyumna and Yuyudhāna, who are considered the two foremost mahārathas among the Vṛṣṇi heroes, are the ones whom you speak of with praise.

sanskrit: नित्यं त्वं कुरुशार्दूल कृष्णश्च मम पुत्रकः ।तावुभौ वृष्णिनाशस्य मुखमास्तां धनंजय ॥७॥
transliteration: nityaṁ tvaṁ kuruśārdūla kṛṣṇaśca mama putrakaḥ ,tāvubhau vṛṣṇināśasya mukhamāstāṁ dhanaṁjaya.
english: O tiger among the Kurus, you, and my son Kṛṣṇa (kṛṣṇa), both of you always became the principal cause of the Vṛṣṇis' destruction, O Dhanañjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. O tiger among the Kurus, you, and my son Kṛṣṇa (kṛṣṇa), both of you always became the principal cause of the Vṛṣṇis' destruction, O Dhanañjaya.

sanskrit: न तु गर्हामि शैनेयं हार्दिक्यं चाहमर्जुन ।अक्रूरं रौक्मिणेयं च शापो ह्येवात्र कारणम् ॥८॥
transliteration: na tu garhāmi śaineyaṁ hārdikyaṁ cāhamarjuna ,akrūraṁ raukmiṇeyaṁ ca śāpo hyevātra kāraṇam.
english: But I, O Arjuna, do not blame the son of Śini (Satyaki) or the son of Hṛdika (Kṛtavarman), nor Akrūra or the son of Rukmiṇī (Pradyumna). Truly, a curse is the cause of this situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. But I, O Arjuna, do not blame the son of Śini (Satyaki) or the son of Hṛdika (Kṛtavarman), nor Akrūra or the son of Rukmiṇī (Pradyumna). Truly, a curse is the cause of this situation.

sanskrit: केशिनं यस्तु कंसं च विक्रम्य जगतः प्रभुः ।विदेहावकरोत्पार्थ चैद्यं च बलगर्वितम् ॥९॥
transliteration: keśinaṁ yastu kaṁsaṁ ca vikramya jagataḥ prabhuḥ ,videhāvakarotpārtha caidyaṁ ca balagarvitam.
english: O Pārtha (Arjuna), the Lord of the world, who, having vanquished Keśi and Kamsa, subjugated the Videhas, and also the Caidya (Śiśupāla), who was arrogant due to his strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. O Pārtha (Arjuna), the Lord of the world, who, having vanquished Keśi and Kamsa, subjugated the Videhas, and also the Caidya (Śiśupāla), who was arrogant due to his strength.

sanskrit: नैषादिमेकलव्यं च चक्रे कालिङ्गमागधान् ।गान्धारान्काशिराजं च मरुभूमौ च पार्थिवान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: naiṣādimekalavyaṁ ca cakre kāliṅgamāgadhān ,gāndhārānkāśirājaṁ ca marubhūmau ca pārthivān.
english: He also subjugated the Niṣāda Ekalavya, the Kāliṅgas and Māgadhas, the Gāndhāras, the King of Kāśī, and the rulers in the desert region.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. He also subjugated the Niṣāda Ekalavya, the Kāliṅgas and Māgadhas, the Gāndhāras, the King of Kāśī, and the rulers in the desert region.

sanskrit: प्राच्यांश्च दाक्षिणात्यांश्च पार्वतीयांस्तथा नृपान् ।सोऽभ्युपेक्षितवानेतमनयं मधुसूदनः ॥११॥
transliteration: prācyāṁśca dākṣiṇātyāṁśca pārvatīyāṁstathā nṛpān ,so'bhyupekṣitavānetamanayaṁ madhusūdanaḥ.
english: And the eastern kings, the southern kings, and likewise the mountain kings – Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) tolerated this injustice.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. And the eastern kings, the southern kings, and likewise the mountain kings – Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) tolerated this injustice.

sanskrit: ततः पुत्रांश्च पौत्रांश्च भ्रातॄनथ सखीनपि ।शयानान्निहतान्दृष्ट्वा ततो मामब्रवीदिदम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ putrāṁśca pautrāṁśca bhrātṝnatha sakhīnapi ,śayānānnihatāndṛṣṭvā tato māmabravīdidam.
english: Then, having seen sons, grandsons, brothers, and also friends lying slain, thereupon he spoke these words to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. Then, having seen sons, grandsons, brothers, and also friends lying slain, thereupon he spoke these words to me.

sanskrit: संप्राप्तोऽद्यायमस्यान्तः कुलस्य पुरुषर्षभ ।आगमिष्यति बीभत्सुरिमां द्वारवतीं पुरीम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: saṁprāpto'dyāyamasyāntaḥ kulasya puruṣarṣabha ,āgamiṣyati bībhatsurimāṁ dvāravatīṁ purīm.
english: O best among men, today this end of this lineage has come. Arjuna will arrive at this city of Dvāravatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. O best among men, today this end of this lineage has come. Arjuna will arrive at this city of Dvāravatī.

sanskrit: आख्येयं तस्य यद्वृत्तं वृष्णीनां वैशसं महत् ।स तु श्रुत्वा महातेजा यदूनामनयं प्रभो ।आगन्ता क्षिप्रमेवेह न मेऽत्रास्ति विचारणा ॥१४॥
transliteration: ākhyeyaṁ tasya yadvṛttaṁ vṛṣṇīnāṁ vaiśasaṁ mahat ,sa tu śrutvā mahātejā yadūnāmanayaṁ prabho ,āgantā kṣiprameveha na me'trāsti vicāraṇā.
english: That great calamity which befell the Vṛṣṇis, whatever happened, should be reported to him. But he, O lord, the greatly glorious one, having heard of the Yadus' misfortune, will certainly come here quickly; there is no doubt in my mind about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. That great calamity which befell the Vṛṣṇis, whatever happened, should be reported to him. But he, O lord, the greatly glorious one, having heard of the Yadus' misfortune, will certainly come here quickly; there is no doubt in my mind about this.

sanskrit: योऽहं तमर्जुनं विद्धि योऽर्जुनः सोऽहमेव तु ।यद्ब्रूयात्तत्तथा कार्यमिति बुध्यस्व माधव ॥१५॥
transliteration: yo'haṁ tamarjunaṁ viddhi yo'rjunaḥ so'hameva tu ,yadbrūyāttattathā kāryamiti budhyasva mādhava.
english: Know that I am Arjuna, and indeed, he who is Arjuna is I myself. Whatever he (Arjuna) may say, that should be carried out accordingly; understand this, O Mādhava.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. Know that I am Arjuna, and indeed, he who is Arjuna is I myself. Whatever he (Arjuna) may say, that should be carried out accordingly; understand this, O Mādhava.

sanskrit: स स्त्रीषु प्राप्तकालं वः पाण्डवो बालकेषु च ।प्रतिपत्स्यति बीभत्सुर्भवतश्चौर्ध्वदेहिकम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa strīṣu prāptakālaṁ vaḥ pāṇḍavo bālakeṣu ca ,pratipatsyati bībhatsurbhavataścaurdhvadehikam.
english: That Pāṇḍava, Arjuna, will perform what is appropriate for your women and children. And Arjuna will also perform your funeral rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. That Pāṇḍava, Arjuna, will perform what is appropriate for your women and children. And Arjuna will also perform your funeral rites.

sanskrit: इमां च नगरीं सद्यः प्रतियाते धनंजये ।प्राकाराट्टालकोपेतां समुद्रः प्लावयिष्यति ॥१७॥
transliteration: imāṁ ca nagarīṁ sadyaḥ pratiyāte dhanaṁjaye ,prākārāṭṭālakopetāṁ samudraḥ plāvayiṣyati.
english: And as soon as Dhanañjaya has departed, the ocean will immediately submerge this city, which is adorned with ramparts and watchtowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. And as soon as Dhanañjaya has departed, the ocean will immediately submerge this city, which is adorned with ramparts and watchtowers.

sanskrit: अहं हि देशे कस्मिंश्चित्पुण्ये नियममास्थितः ।कालं कर्ता सद्य एव रामेण सह धीमता ॥१८॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hi deśe kasmiṁścitpuṇye niyamamāsthitaḥ ,kālaṁ kartā sadya eva rāmeṇa saha dhīmatā.
english: Indeed, having undertaken a religious observance (niyama) in some sacred place, I will very soon conclude my time (on earth) along with the wise Rāma.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. Indeed, having undertaken a religious observance (niyama) in some sacred place, I will very soon conclude my time (on earth) along with the wise Rāma.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशो मामचिन्त्यपराक्रमः ।हित्वा मां बालकैः सार्धं दिशं कामप्यगात्प्रभुः ॥१९॥
transliteration: evamuktvā hṛṣīkeśo māmacintyaparākramaḥ ,hitvā māṁ bālakaiḥ sārdhaṁ diśaṁ kāmapyagātprabhuḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Hṛṣīkeśa, whose valor is inconceivable, leaving me along with the children, the Lord departed towards some unknown direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. Having spoken thus, Hṛṣīkeśa, whose valor is inconceivable, leaving me along with the children, the Lord departed towards some unknown direction.

sanskrit: सोऽहं तौ च महात्मानौ चिन्तयन्भ्रातरौ तव ।घोरं ज्ञातिवधं चैव न भुञ्जे शोककर्शितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: so'haṁ tau ca mahātmānau cintayanbhrātarau tava ,ghoraṁ jñātivadhaṁ caiva na bhuñje śokakarśitaḥ.
english: That I, meditating on those two great-souled brothers of yours, and also on the terrible slaughter of kinsmen (jñātivadham), being emaciated by grief, do not enjoy food.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. That I, meditating on those two great-souled brothers of yours, and also on the terrible slaughter of kinsmen (jñātivadham), being emaciated by grief, do not enjoy food.

sanskrit: न च भोक्ष्ये न जीविष्ये दिष्ट्या प्राप्तोऽसि पाण्डव ।यदुक्तं पार्थ कृष्णेन तत्सर्वमखिलं कुरु ॥२१॥
transliteration: na ca bhokṣye na jīviṣye diṣṭyā prāpto'si pāṇḍava ,yaduktaṁ pārtha kṛṣṇena tatsarvamakhilaṁ kuru.
english: I shall neither enjoy (anything) nor live. Fortunately, O Pāṇḍava, you have arrived! O Pārtha, carry out completely all that Kṛṣṇa has instructed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 21. I shall neither enjoy (anything) nor live. Fortunately, O Pāṇḍava, you have arrived! O Pārtha, carry out completely all that Kṛṣṇa has instructed.

sanskrit: एतत्ते पार्थ राज्यं च स्त्रियो रत्नानि चैव ह ।इष्टान्प्राणानहं हीमांस्त्यक्ष्यामि रिपुसूदन ॥२२॥
transliteration: etatte pārtha rājyaṁ ca striyo ratnāni caiva ha ,iṣṭānprāṇānahaṁ hīmāṁstyakṣyāmi ripusūdana.
english: O Pārtha, this kingdom, these women, and jewels are all for you. Indeed, O destroyer of enemies, I shall give up even these dear lives (prāṇa) (of mine).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 7 Verse 22. O Pārtha, this kingdom, these women, and jewels are all for you. Indeed, O destroyer of enemies, I shall give up even these dear lives (prāṇa) (of mine).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तः स बीभत्सुर्मातुलेन परंतपः ।दुर्मना दीनमनसं वसुदेवमुवाच ह ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktaḥ sa bībhatsurmātulena paraṁtapaḥ ,durmanā dīnamanasaṁ vasudevamuvāca ha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed by his maternal uncle, that Arjuna (Bībhatsu), the tormentor of foes, being dejected in mind, then spoke to the sorrowful Vasudeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed by his maternal uncle, that Arjuna (Bībhatsu), the tormentor of foes, being dejected in mind, then spoke to the sorrowful Vasudeva.

sanskrit: नाहं वृष्णिप्रवीरेण मधुभिश्चैव मातुल ।विहीनां पृथिवीं द्रष्टुं शक्तश्चिरमिह प्रभो ॥२॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ vṛṣṇipravīreṇa madhubhiścaiva mātula ,vihīnāṁ pṛthivīṁ draṣṭuṁ śaktaściramiha prabho.
english: O maternal uncle, O lord, I am not able to endure seeing this earth without the chief of the Vṛṣṇis (Kṛṣṇa) and the Madhus (Yādavas) for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. O maternal uncle, O lord, I am not able to endure seeing this earth without the chief of the Vṛṣṇis (Kṛṣṇa) and the Madhus (Yādavas) for a long time.

sanskrit: राजा च भीमसेनश्च सहदेवश्च पाण्डवः ।नकुलो याज्ञसेनी च षडेकमनसो वयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: rājā ca bhīmasenaśca sahadevaśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,nakulo yājñasenī ca ṣaḍekamanaso vayam.
english: The King, Bhīmasena, Sahadeva the Pāṇḍava, Nakula, and Yājñasenī – we six are of one mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. The King, Bhīmasena, Sahadeva the Pāṇḍava, Nakula, and Yājñasenī – we six are of one mind.

sanskrit: राज्ञः संक्रमणे चापि कालोऽयं वर्तते ध्रुवम् ।तमिमं विद्धि संप्राप्तं कालं कालविदां वर ॥४॥
transliteration: rājñaḥ saṁkramaṇe cāpi kālo'yaṁ vartate dhruvam ,tamimaṁ viddhi saṁprāptaṁ kālaṁ kālavidāṁ vara.
english: Certainly, this is the time of the king's transition. O best among those who know time, know that this time has fully arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. Certainly, this is the time of the king's transition. O best among those who know time, know that this time has fully arrived.

sanskrit: सर्वथा वृष्णिदारांस्तु बालवृद्धांस्तथैव च ।नयिष्ये परिगृह्याहमिन्द्रप्रस्थमरिंदम ॥५॥
transliteration: sarvathā vṛṣṇidārāṁstu bālavṛddhāṁstathaiva ca ,nayiṣye parigṛhyāhamindraprasthamariṁdama.
english: In any case, O subduer of enemies (arindama), I will certainly take the Vṛṣṇi women, children, and elderly, and lead them to Indraprastha.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. In any case, O subduer of enemies (arindama), I will certainly take the Vṛṣṇi women, children, and elderly, and lead them to Indraprastha.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा दारुकमिदं वाक्यमाह धनंजयः ।अमात्यान्वृष्णिवीराणां द्रष्टुमिच्छामि माचिरम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ityuktvā dārukamidaṁ vākyamāha dhanaṁjayaḥ ,amātyānvṛṣṇivīrāṇāṁ draṣṭumicchāmi māciram.
english: Having said this, Dhanañjaya spoke this statement to Dāruka: 'I wish to see the ministers of the Vṛṣṇi heroes without delay.'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. Having said this, Dhanañjaya spoke this statement to Dāruka: 'I wish to see the ministers of the Vṛṣṇi heroes without delay.'

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा वचनं सुधर्मां यादवीं सभाम् ।प्रविवेशार्जुनः शूरः शोचमानो महारथान् ॥७॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā vacanaṁ sudharmāṁ yādavīṁ sabhām ,praviveśārjunaḥ śūraḥ śocamāno mahārathān.
english: Having spoken thus, the heroic Arjuna entered the Sudharmā assembly hall of the Yadus, grieving for the great warriors (mahārathas).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. Having spoken thus, the heroic Arjuna entered the Sudharmā assembly hall of the Yadus, grieving for the great warriors (mahārathas).

sanskrit: तमासनगतं तत्र सर्वाः प्रकृतयस्तथा ।ब्राह्मणा नैगमाश्चैव परिवार्योपतस्थिरे ॥८॥
transliteration: tamāsanagataṁ tatra sarvāḥ prakṛtayastathā ,brāhmaṇā naigamāścaiva parivāryopatasthire.
english: There, all the citizens (prakṛtis), as well as brahmins and merchants, surrounded him, who was seated, and stood respectfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. There, all the citizens (prakṛtis), as well as brahmins and merchants, surrounded him, who was seated, and stood respectfully.

sanskrit: तान्दीनमनसः सर्वान्निभृतान्गतचेतसः ।उवाचेदं वचः पार्थः स्वयं दीनतरस्तदा ॥९॥
transliteration: tāndīnamanasaḥ sarvānnibhṛtāngatacetasaḥ ,uvācedaṁ vacaḥ pārthaḥ svayaṁ dīnatarastadā.
english: Then Pārtha, himself even more distressed, spoke these words to all of them, who were dejected, silent, and disheartened.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. Then Pārtha, himself even more distressed, spoke these words to all of them, who were dejected, silent, and disheartened.

sanskrit: शक्रप्रस्थमहं नेष्ये वृष्ण्यन्धकजनं स्वयम् ।इदं तु नगरं सर्वं समुद्रः प्लावयिष्यति ॥१०॥
transliteration: śakraprasthamahaṁ neṣye vṛṣṇyandhakajanaṁ svayam ,idaṁ tu nagaraṁ sarvaṁ samudraḥ plāvayiṣyati.
english: I myself will lead the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka people to Śakraprastha, but the ocean will inundate this entire city.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. I myself will lead the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka people to Śakraprastha, but the ocean will inundate this entire city.

sanskrit: सज्जीकुरुत यानानि रत्नानि विविधानि च ।वज्रोऽयं भवतां राजा शक्रप्रस्थे भविष्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: sajjīkuruta yānāni ratnāni vividhāni ca ,vajro'yaṁ bhavatāṁ rājā śakraprasthe bhaviṣyati.
english: Prepare the conveyances and various jewels, for this Vajra will be your king in Śakraprastha.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. Prepare the conveyances and various jewels, for this Vajra will be your king in Śakraprastha.

sanskrit: सप्तमे दिवसे चैव रवौ विमल उद्गते ।बहिर्वत्स्यामहे सर्वे सज्जीभवत माचिरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: saptame divase caiva ravau vimala udgate ,bahirvatsyāmahe sarve sajjībhavata māciram.
english: On the seventh day, when the pure sun has risen, we shall all reside outside. Prepare yourselves without delay.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. On the seventh day, when the pure sun has risen, we shall all reside outside. Prepare yourselves without delay.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तास्तेन ते पौराः पार्थेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।सज्जमाशु ततश्चक्रुः स्वसिद्ध्यर्थं समुत्सुकाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ityuktāstena te paurāḥ pārthenākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,sajjamāśu tataścakruḥ svasiddhyarthaṁ samutsukāḥ.
english: Thus spoken to by Pārtha (Arjuna), whose actions (karma) were unblemished, those citizens, eager for their own success, quickly made the arrangements.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. Thus spoken to by Pārtha (Arjuna), whose actions (karma) were unblemished, those citizens, eager for their own success, quickly made the arrangements.

sanskrit: तां रात्रिमवसत्पार्थः केशवस्य निवेशने ।महता शोकमोहेन सहसाभिपरिप्लुतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tāṁ rātrimavasatpārthaḥ keśavasya niveśane ,mahatā śokamohena sahasābhipariplutaḥ.
english: That night, Pārtha (Arjuna) stayed in Keśava's dwelling, suddenly overcome by great sorrow and mental bewilderment (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. That night, Pārtha (Arjuna) stayed in Keśava's dwelling, suddenly overcome by great sorrow and mental bewilderment (moha).

sanskrit: श्वोभूतेऽथ ततः शौरिर्वसुदेवः प्रतापवान् ।युक्त्वात्मानं महातेजा जगाम गतिमुत्तमाम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: śvobhūte'tha tataḥ śaurirvasudevaḥ pratāpavān ,yuktvātmānaṁ mahātejā jagāma gatimuttamām.
english: On the following day, then, the mighty and greatly effulgent Vasudeva, son of Śūra, having concentrated his inner self (ātman), attained the supreme destination.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. On the following day, then, the mighty and greatly effulgent Vasudeva, son of Śūra, having concentrated his inner self (ātman), attained the supreme destination.

sanskrit: ततः शब्दो महानासीद्वसुदेवस्य वेश्मनि ।दारुणः क्रोशतीनां च रुदतीनां च योषिताम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ śabdo mahānāsīdvasudevasya veśmani ,dāruṇaḥ krośatīnāṁ ca rudatīnāṁ ca yoṣitām.
english: Then there was a great and dreadful sound in Vasudeva's house, the crying and weeping of women.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. Then there was a great and dreadful sound in Vasudeva's house, the crying and weeping of women.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णमूर्धजाः सर्वा विमुक्ताभरणस्रजः ।उरांसि पाणिभिर्घ्नन्त्यो व्यलपन्करुणं स्त्रियः ॥१७॥
transliteration: prakīrṇamūrdhajāḥ sarvā vimuktābharaṇasrajaḥ ,urāṁsi pāṇibhirghnantyo vyalapankaruṇaṁ striyaḥ.
english: All the women, with dishevelled hair and loosened ornaments and garlands, lamented piteously while striking their chests with their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. All the women, with dishevelled hair and loosened ornaments and garlands, lamented piteously while striking their chests with their hands.

sanskrit: तं देवकी च भद्रा च रोहिणी मदिरा तथा ।अन्वारोढुं व्यवसिता भर्तारं योषितां वराः ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ devakī ca bhadrā ca rohiṇī madirā tathā ,anvāroḍhuṁ vyavasitā bhartāraṁ yoṣitāṁ varāḥ.
english: Devaki, Bhadrā, Rohiṇī, and Madirā, who were the foremost among women, resolved to follow their husband (onto the funeral pyre).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. Devaki, Bhadrā, Rohiṇī, and Madirā, who were the foremost among women, resolved to follow their husband (onto the funeral pyre).

sanskrit: ततः शौरिं नृयुक्तेन बहुमाल्येन भारत ।यानेन महता पार्थो बहिर्निष्क्रामयत्तदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śauriṁ nṛyuktena bahumālyena bhārata ,yānena mahatā pārtho bahirniṣkrāmayattadā.
english: Then, O Bhārata, Arjuna (Pārtha) escorted Krishna (Śauri) out in a magnificent, man-drawn vehicle adorned with many garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. Then, O Bhārata, Arjuna (Pārtha) escorted Krishna (Śauri) out in a magnificent, man-drawn vehicle adorned with many garlands.

sanskrit: तमन्वयुस्तत्र तत्र दुःखशोकसमाहताः ।द्वारकावासिनः पौराः सर्व एव नरर्षभ ॥२०॥
transliteration: tamanvayustatra tatra duḥkhaśokasamāhatāḥ ,dvārakāvāsinaḥ paurāḥ sarva eva nararṣabha.
english: O best among men (nararṣabha), all the citizens residing in Dvārakā, overwhelmed with sorrow and grief, followed him there.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. O best among men (nararṣabha), all the citizens residing in Dvārakā, overwhelmed with sorrow and grief, followed him there.

sanskrit: तस्याश्वमेधिकं छत्रं दीप्यमानाश्च पावकाः ।पुरस्तात्तस्य यानस्य याजकाश्च ततो ययुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasyāśvamedhikaṁ chatraṁ dīpyamānāśca pāvakāḥ ,purastāttasya yānasya yājakāśca tato yayuḥ.
english: His royal umbrella (aśvamedhikaṃ chatraṃ), associated with the aśvamedha, and blazing sacred fires, along with the priests (yājaka), then proceeded in front of his vehicle.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. His royal umbrella (aśvamedhikaṃ chatraṃ), associated with the aśvamedha, and blazing sacred fires, along with the priests (yājaka), then proceeded in front of his vehicle.

sanskrit: अनुजग्मुश्च तं वीरं देव्यस्ता वै स्वलंकृताः ।स्त्रीसहस्रैः परिवृता वधूभिश्च सहस्रशः ॥२२॥
transliteration: anujagmuśca taṁ vīraṁ devyastā vai svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,strīsahasraiḥ parivṛtā vadhūbhiśca sahasraśaḥ.
english: And those well-adorned royal ladies (devī), surrounded by thousands of women and thousands of brides (vadhū), indeed followed that hero.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. And those well-adorned royal ladies (devī), surrounded by thousands of women and thousands of brides (vadhū), indeed followed that hero.

sanskrit: यस्तु देशः प्रियस्तस्य जीवतोऽभून्महात्मनः ।तत्रैनमुपसंकल्प्य पितृमेधं प्रचक्रिरे ॥२३॥
transliteration: yastu deśaḥ priyastasya jīvato'bhūnmahātmanaḥ ,tatrainamupasaṁkalpya pitṛmedhaṁ pracakrire.
english: The noble persons performed the ancestral ritual (pitṛmedha) in the very place that had been dear to him during his lifetime, having first made all the necessary arrangements.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. The noble persons performed the ancestral ritual (pitṛmedha) in the very place that had been dear to him during his lifetime, having first made all the necessary arrangements.

sanskrit: तं चिताग्निगतं वीरं शूरपुत्रं वराङ्गनाः ।ततोऽन्वारुरुहुः पत्न्यश्चतस्रः पतिलोकगाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: taṁ citāgnigataṁ vīraṁ śūraputraṁ varāṅganāḥ ,tato'nvāruruhuḥ patnyaścatasraḥ patilokagāḥ.
english: Then, four excellent women, his wives, who were destined to follow him to the husband's realm (patiloka), ascended after that hero, the son of Śūra, who had entered the funeral pyre.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. Then, four excellent women, his wives, who were destined to follow him to the husband's realm (patiloka), ascended after that hero, the son of Śūra, who had entered the funeral pyre.

sanskrit: तं वै चतसृभिः स्त्रीभिरन्वितं पाण्डुनन्दनः ।अदाहयच्चन्दनैश्च गन्धैरुच्चावचैरपि ॥२५॥
transliteration: taṁ vai catasṛbhiḥ strībhiranvitaṁ pāṇḍunandanaḥ ,adāhayaccandanaiśca gandhairuccāvacairapi.
english: Indeed, the son of Pāṇḍu cremated him, accompanied by the four women, using sandalwood and various fragrances.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. Indeed, the son of Pāṇḍu cremated him, accompanied by the four women, using sandalwood and various fragrances.

sanskrit: ततः प्रादुरभूच्छब्दः समिद्धस्य विभावसोः ।सामगानां च निर्घोषो नराणां रुदतामपि ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ prādurabhūcchabdaḥ samiddhasya vibhāvasoḥ ,sāmagānāṁ ca nirghoṣo narāṇāṁ rudatāmapi.
english: Then, the sound of the blazing fire became manifest, as did the chanting of the Sāma Veda singers, and the wailing of the men.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. Then, the sound of the blazing fire became manifest, as did the chanting of the Sāma Veda singers, and the wailing of the men.

sanskrit: ततो वज्रप्रधानास्ते वृष्णिवीरकुमारकाः ।सर्व एवोदकं चक्रुः स्त्रियश्चैव महात्मनः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato vajrapradhānāste vṛṣṇivīrakumārakāḥ ,sarva evodakaṁ cakruḥ striyaścaiva mahātmanaḥ.
english: Then, all those heroic young men of the Vṛṣṇi clan, led by Vajra, and also the women, performed the water ritual for the departed great-souled ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. Then, all those heroic young men of the Vṛṣṇi clan, led by Vajra, and also the women, performed the water ritual for the departed great-souled ones.

sanskrit: अलुप्तधर्मस्तं धर्मं कारयित्वा स फल्गुनः ।जगाम वृष्णयो यत्र विनष्टा भरतर्षभ ॥२८॥
transliteration: aluptadharmastaṁ dharmaṁ kārayitvā sa phalgunaḥ ,jagāma vṛṣṇayo yatra vinaṣṭā bharatarṣabha.
english: That Arjuna (Phalguna), whose sense of natural law (dharma) was unfailing, having caused that ritual (dharma) to be performed, then went to the place where the Vṛṣṇis had perished, O best of Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. That Arjuna (Phalguna), whose sense of natural law (dharma) was unfailing, having caused that ritual (dharma) to be performed, then went to the place where the Vṛṣṇis had perished, O best of Bharatas.

sanskrit: स तान्दृष्ट्वा निपतितान्कदने भृशदुःखितः ।बभूवातीव कौरव्यः प्राप्तकालं चकार च ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa tāndṛṣṭvā nipatitānkadane bhṛśaduḥkhitaḥ ,babhūvātīva kauravyaḥ prāptakālaṁ cakāra ca.
english: Having seen them fallen in the slaughter, that descendant of Kuru (Arjuna) became exceedingly and deeply distressed, and he then performed what was appropriate for the occasion.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. Having seen them fallen in the slaughter, that descendant of Kuru (Arjuna) became exceedingly and deeply distressed, and he then performed what was appropriate for the occasion.

sanskrit: यथाप्रधानतश्चैव चक्रे सर्वाः क्रियास्तदा ।ये हता ब्रह्मशापेन मुसलैरेरकोद्भवैः ॥३०॥
transliteration: yathāpradhānataścaiva cakre sarvāḥ kriyāstadā ,ye hatā brahmaśāpena musalairerakodbhavaiḥ.
english: Then, he performed all the rites according to their status, for those who had been slain by the maces arisen from era grass, due to the curse of the Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. Then, he performed all the rites according to their status, for those who had been slain by the maces arisen from era grass, due to the curse of the Brahmins.

sanskrit: ततः शरीरे रामस्य वासुदेवस्य चोभयोः ।अन्विष्य दाहयामास पुरुषैराप्तकारिभिः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarīre rāmasya vāsudevasya cobhayoḥ ,anviṣya dāhayāmāsa puruṣairāptakāribhiḥ.
english: Then, having located the bodies of both Rama and Vasudeva (Krishna), he (Arjuna) had them cremated by trustworthy men.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. Then, having located the bodies of both Rama and Vasudeva (Krishna), he (Arjuna) had them cremated by trustworthy men.

sanskrit: स तेषां विधिवत्कृत्वा प्रेतकार्याणि पाण्डवः ।सप्तमे दिवसे प्रायाद्रथमारुह्य सत्वरः ।अश्वयुक्तै रथैश्चापि गोखरोष्ट्रयुतैरपि ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa teṣāṁ vidhivatkṛtvā pretakāryāṇi pāṇḍavaḥ ,saptame divase prāyādrathamāruhya satvaraḥ ,aśvayuktai rathaiścāpi gokharoṣṭrayutairapi.
english: That son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), having duly performed their funeral rites, departed swiftly on the seventh day, mounting a chariot. He was accompanied by other horse-drawn chariots, as well as those drawn by cows, donkeys, and camels.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. That son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), having duly performed their funeral rites, departed swiftly on the seventh day, mounting a chariot. He was accompanied by other horse-drawn chariots, as well as those drawn by cows, donkeys, and camels.

sanskrit: स्त्रियस्ता वृष्णिवीराणां रुदत्यः शोककर्शिताः ।अनुजग्मुर्महात्मानं पाण्डुपुत्रं धनंजयम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: striyastā vṛṣṇivīrāṇāṁ rudatyaḥ śokakarśitāḥ ,anujagmurmahātmānaṁ pāṇḍuputraṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: Those women of the Vṛṣṇi heroes, weeping and emaciated by sorrow, followed the great-souled (mahātman) son of Pāṇḍu, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. Those women of the Vṛṣṇi heroes, weeping and emaciated by sorrow, followed the great-souled (mahātman) son of Pāṇḍu, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: भृत्यास्त्वन्धकवृष्णीनां सादिनो रथिनश्च ये ।वीरहीनं वृद्धबालं पौरजानपदास्तथा ।ययुस्ते परिवार्याथ कलत्रं पार्थशासनात् ॥३४॥
transliteration: bhṛtyāstvandhakavṛṣṇīnāṁ sādino rathinaśca ye ,vīrahīnaṁ vṛddhabālaṁ paurajānapadāstathā ,yayuste parivāryātha kalatraṁ pārthaśāsanāt.
english: And those servants of the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis, the horsemen and charioteers, along with the citizens and country folk - all those left without heroes, both old and young - then proceeded, escorting the women and dependents (kalatra), by the command of Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. And those servants of the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis, the horsemen and charioteers, along with the citizens and country folk - all those left without heroes, both old and young - then proceeded, escorting the women and dependents (kalatra), by the command of Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: कुञ्जरैश्च गजारोहा ययुः शैलनिभैस्तथा ।सपादरक्षैः संयुक्ताः सोत्तरायुधिका ययुः ॥३५॥
transliteration: kuñjaraiśca gajārohā yayuḥ śailanibhaistathā ,sapādarakṣaiḥ saṁyuktāḥ sottarāyudhikā yayuḥ.
english: Elephant riders, mounted on mountain-like elephants, departed, accompanied by foot-guards and those bearing additional weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. Elephant riders, mounted on mountain-like elephants, departed, accompanied by foot-guards and those bearing additional weapons.

sanskrit: पुत्राश्चान्धकवृष्णीनां सर्वे पार्थमनुव्रताः ।ब्राह्मणाः क्षत्रिया वैश्याः शूद्राश्चैव महाधनाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: putrāścāndhakavṛṣṇīnāṁ sarve pārthamanuvratāḥ ,brāhmaṇāḥ kṣatriyā vaiśyāḥ śūdrāścaiva mahādhanāḥ.
english: All the sons of the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis, devoted to Pārtha (Arjuna), along with Brahmins, Kṣatriyas, Vaiśyas, and even wealthy Śūdras, also followed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 36. All the sons of the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis, devoted to Pārtha (Arjuna), along with Brahmins, Kṣatriyas, Vaiśyas, and even wealthy Śūdras, also followed.

sanskrit: दश षट्च सहस्राणि वासुदेवावरोधनम् ।पुरस्कृत्य ययुर्वज्रं पौत्रं कृष्णस्य धीमतः ॥३७॥
transliteration: daśa ṣaṭca sahasrāṇi vāsudevāvarodhanam ,puraskṛtya yayurvajraṁ pautraṁ kṛṣṇasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Sixteen thousand women from Vasudeva's inner apartments departed, placing Vajra, the grandson of the wise (Kṛṣṇa), at their forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 37. Sixteen thousand women from Vasudeva's inner apartments departed, placing Vajra, the grandson of the wise (Kṛṣṇa), at their forefront.

sanskrit: बहूनि च सहस्राणि प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि च ।भोजवृष्ण्यन्धकस्त्रीणां हतनाथानि निर्ययुः ॥३८॥
transliteration: bahūni ca sahasrāṇi prayutānyarbudāni ca ,bhojavṛṣṇyandhakastrīṇāṁ hatanāthāni niryayuḥ.
english: Many thousands, tens of thousands (prayuta), and even tens of millions (arbuda) of women belonging to the Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, and Andhakas, whose protectors had been slain, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 38. Many thousands, tens of thousands (prayuta), and even tens of millions (arbuda) of women belonging to the Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, and Andhakas, whose protectors had been slain, departed.

sanskrit: तत्सागरसमप्रख्यं वृष्णिचक्रं महर्द्धिमत् ।उवाह रथिनां श्रेष्ठः पार्थः परपुरंजयः ॥३९॥
transliteration: tatsāgarasamaprakhyaṁ vṛṣṇicakraṁ maharddhimat ,uvāha rathināṁ śreṣṭhaḥ pārthaḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ.
english: The best among charioteers, Arjuna (pārtha), conqueror of enemy cities, carried that mighty multitude of Vrishnis, which resembled the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 39. The best among charioteers, Arjuna (pārtha), conqueror of enemy cities, carried that mighty multitude of Vrishnis, which resembled the ocean.

sanskrit: निर्याते तु जने तस्मिन्सागरो मकरालयः ।द्वारकां रत्नसंपूर्णां जलेनाप्लावयत्तदा ॥४०॥
transliteration: niryāte tu jane tasminsāgaro makarālayaḥ ,dvārakāṁ ratnasaṁpūrṇāṁ jalenāplāvayattadā.
english: But when those people had departed, the ocean (sāgara), the abode of marine creatures, then inundated Dvārakā, which was full of jewels, with its waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 40. But when those people had departed, the ocean (sāgara), the abode of marine creatures, then inundated Dvārakā, which was full of jewels, with its waters.

sanskrit: तदद्भुतमभिप्रेक्ष्य द्वारकावासिनो जनाः ।तूर्णात्तूर्णतरं जग्मुरहो दैवमिति ब्रुवन् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tadadbhutamabhiprekṣya dvārakāvāsino janāḥ ,tūrṇāttūrṇataraṁ jagmuraho daivamiti bruvan.
english: Having witnessed that astonishing event, the residents of Dvārakā departed more quickly than ever, exclaiming, 'Oh, this is destiny (daiva)!'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 41. Having witnessed that astonishing event, the residents of Dvārakā departed more quickly than ever, exclaiming, 'Oh, this is destiny (daiva)!'

sanskrit: काननेषु च रम्येषु पर्वतेषु नदीषु च ।निवसन्नानयामास वृष्णिदारान्धनंजयः ॥४२॥
transliteration: kānaneṣu ca ramyeṣu parvateṣu nadīṣu ca ,nivasannānayāmāsa vṛṣṇidārāndhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: While dwelling in beautiful forests, on mountains, and near rivers, Arjuna (dhanañjaya) led the wives of the Vrishnis.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 42. While dwelling in beautiful forests, on mountains, and near rivers, Arjuna (dhanañjaya) led the wives of the Vrishnis.

sanskrit: स पञ्चनदमासाद्य धीमानतिसमृद्धिमत् ।देशे गोपशुधान्याढ्ये निवासमकरोत्प्रभुः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sa pañcanadamāsādya dhīmānatisamṛddhimat ,deśe gopaśudhānyāḍhye nivāsamakarotprabhuḥ.
english: Having reached Pañcanada, a region that was extremely prosperous and abundant in cows, cattle, and grains, that wise and powerful lord established his residence there.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 43. Having reached Pañcanada, a region that was extremely prosperous and abundant in cows, cattle, and grains, that wise and powerful lord established his residence there.

sanskrit: ततो लोभः समभवद्दस्यूनां निहतेश्वराः ।दृष्ट्वा स्त्रियो नीयमानाः पार्थेनैकेन भारत ॥४४॥
transliteration: tato lobhaḥ samabhavaddasyūnāṁ nihateśvarāḥ ,dṛṣṭvā striyo nīyamānāḥ pārthenaikena bhārata.
english: Then, O Bhārata, the robbers, observing the women whose lords had been slain being led away by Arjuna (Pārtha) alone, became filled with greed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 44. Then, O Bhārata, the robbers, observing the women whose lords had been slain being led away by Arjuna (Pārtha) alone, became filled with greed.

sanskrit: ततस्ते पापकर्माणो लोभोपहतचेतसः ।आभीरा मन्त्रयामासुः समेत्याशुभदर्शनाः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tataste pāpakarmāṇo lobhopahatacetasaḥ ,ābhīrā mantrayāmāsuḥ sametyāśubhadarśanāḥ.
english: Then, those Abhiras, the evildoers whose minds were overcome by greed and who possessed an inauspicious appearance, assembled and deliberated.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 45. Then, those Abhiras, the evildoers whose minds were overcome by greed and who possessed an inauspicious appearance, assembled and deliberated.

sanskrit: अयमेकोऽर्जुनो योद्धा वृद्धबालं हतेश्वरम् ।नयत्यस्मानतिक्रम्य योधाश्चेमे हतौजसः ॥४६॥
transliteration: ayameko'rjuno yoddhā vṛddhabālaṁ hateśvaram ,nayatyasmānatikramya yodhāśceme hataujasaḥ.
english: This single warrior, Arjuna, is taking away the old and the young, whose protectors have been slain, by overcoming us. And these (remaining) warriors are utterly devoid of energy.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 46. This single warrior, Arjuna, is taking away the old and the young, whose protectors have been slain, by overcoming us. And these (remaining) warriors are utterly devoid of energy.

sanskrit: ततो यष्टिप्रहरणा दस्यवस्ते सहस्रशः ।अभ्यधावन्त वृष्णीनां तं जनं लोप्त्रहारिणः ॥४७॥
transliteration: tato yaṣṭipraharaṇā dasyavaste sahasraśaḥ ,abhyadhāvanta vṛṣṇīnāṁ taṁ janaṁ loptrahāriṇaḥ.
english: Then, those robbers, armed with staffs and intent on plunder, rushed by thousands towards that multitude of the Vṛṣṇis.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 47. Then, those robbers, armed with staffs and intent on plunder, rushed by thousands towards that multitude of the Vṛṣṇis.

sanskrit: महता सिंहनादेन द्रावयन्तः पृथग्जनम् ।अभिपेतुर्धनार्थं ते कालपर्यायचोदिताः ॥४८॥
transliteration: mahatā siṁhanādena drāvayantaḥ pṛthagjanam ,abhipeturdhanārthaṁ te kālaparyāyacoditāḥ.
english: Scattering the common people with a mighty lion's roar, they rushed forth for wealth, impelled by the course of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 48. Scattering the common people with a mighty lion's roar, they rushed forth for wealth, impelled by the course of time.

sanskrit: ततो निवृत्तः कौन्तेयः सहसा सपदानुगः ।उवाच तान्महाबाहुरर्जुनः प्रहसन्निव ॥४९॥
transliteration: tato nivṛttaḥ kaunteyaḥ sahasā sapadānugaḥ ,uvāca tānmahābāhurarjunaḥ prahasanniva.
english: Then Arjuna, the mighty-armed son of Kunti, swiftly turned back with his followers and addressed them as if with a laugh.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 49. Then Arjuna, the mighty-armed son of Kunti, swiftly turned back with his followers and addressed them as if with a laugh.

sanskrit: निवर्तध्वमधर्मज्ञा यदि स्थ न मुमूर्षवः ।नेदानीं शरनिर्भिन्नाः शोचध्वं निहता मया ॥५०॥
transliteration: nivartadhvamadharmajñā yadi stha na mumūrṣavaḥ ,nedānīṁ śaranirbhinnāḥ śocadhvaṁ nihatā mayā.
english: Turn back, you who are ignorant of natural law (dharma), if you do not wish to die! For you will not grieve then, when you are pierced by arrows and killed by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 50. Turn back, you who are ignorant of natural law (dharma), if you do not wish to die! For you will not grieve then, when you are pierced by arrows and killed by me.

sanskrit: तथोक्तास्तेन वीरेण कदर्थीकृत्य तद्वचः ।अभिपेतुर्जनं मूढा वार्यमाणाः पुनः पुनः ॥५१॥
transliteration: tathoktāstena vīreṇa kadarthīkṛtya tadvacaḥ ,abhipeturjanaṁ mūḍhā vāryamāṇāḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Although thus addressed by that hero, those foolish ones, disregarding his words, repeatedly rushed towards the people, even as they were being restrained again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 51. Although thus addressed by that hero, those foolish ones, disregarding his words, repeatedly rushed towards the people, even as they were being restrained again and again.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो धनुर्दिव्यं गाण्डीवमजरं महत् ।आरोपयितुमारेभे यत्नादिव कथंचन ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno dhanurdivyaṁ gāṇḍīvamajaraṁ mahat ,āropayitumārebhe yatnādiva kathaṁcana.
english: Then Arjuna began, as if with immense effort and somehow, to string the divine, imperishable, great bow named Gaṇḍīva.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 52. Then Arjuna began, as if with immense effort and somehow, to string the divine, imperishable, great bow named Gaṇḍīva.

sanskrit: चकार सज्यं कृच्छ्रेण संभ्रमे तुमुले सति ।चिन्तयामास चास्त्राणि न च सस्मार तान्यपि ॥५३॥
transliteration: cakāra sajyaṁ kṛcchreṇa saṁbhrame tumule sati ,cintayāmāsa cāstrāṇi na ca sasmāra tānyapi.
english: He strung it with great difficulty amidst the tumultuous confusion. And he contemplated the divine missiles (astrāṇi), but he could not even recall them.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 53. He strung it with great difficulty amidst the tumultuous confusion. And he contemplated the divine missiles (astrāṇi), but he could not even recall them.

sanskrit: वैकृत्यं तन्महद्दृष्ट्वा भुजवीर्ये तथा युधि ।दिव्यानां च महास्त्राणां विनाशाद्व्रीडितोऽभवत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: vaikṛtyaṁ tanmahaddṛṣṭvā bhujavīrye tathā yudhi ,divyānāṁ ca mahāstrāṇāṁ vināśādvrīḍito'bhavat.
english: Having witnessed that great deterioration in his arm-strength and in battle, and the loss of his divine great missiles (mahāstrāṇi), he became ashamed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 54. Having witnessed that great deterioration in his arm-strength and in battle, and the loss of his divine great missiles (mahāstrāṇi), he became ashamed.

sanskrit: वृष्णियोधाश्च ते सर्वे गजाश्वरथयायिनः ।न शेकुरावर्तयितुं ह्रियमाणं च तं जनम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: vṛṣṇiyodhāśca te sarve gajāśvarathayāyinaḥ ,na śekurāvartayituṁ hriyamāṇaṁ ca taṁ janam.
english: All those Vrishni warriors, though mounted on elephants, horses, and chariots, were unable to retrieve the people who were being carried away.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 55. All those Vrishni warriors, though mounted on elephants, horses, and chariots, were unable to retrieve the people who were being carried away.

sanskrit: कलत्रस्य बहुत्वात्तु संपतत्सु ततस्ततः ।प्रयत्नमकरोत्पार्थो जनस्य परिरक्षणे ॥५६॥
transliteration: kalatrasya bahutvāttu saṁpatatsu tatastataḥ ,prayatnamakarotpārtho janasya parirakṣaṇe.
english: But because of the large number of women scattering in every direction, Arjuna (Pārtha) made an effort to protect the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 56. But because of the large number of women scattering in every direction, Arjuna (Pārtha) made an effort to protect the people.

sanskrit: मिषतां सर्वयोधानां ततस्ताः प्रमदोत्तमाः ।समन्ततोऽवकृष्यन्त कामाच्चान्याः प्रवव्रजुः ॥५७॥
transliteration: miṣatāṁ sarvayodhānāṁ tatastāḥ pramadottamāḥ ,samantato'vakṛṣyanta kāmāccānyāḥ pravavrajuḥ.
english: Then, while all the warriors watched, those excellent women were dragged away from all sides, and others departed willingly out of desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 57. Then, while all the warriors watched, those excellent women were dragged away from all sides, and others departed willingly out of desire.

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैः शरैः पार्थो धनंजयः ।जघान दस्यून्सोद्वेगो वृष्णिभृत्यैः सह प्रभुः ॥५८॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvanirmuktaiḥ śaraiḥ pārtho dhanaṁjayaḥ ,jaghāna dasyūnsodvego vṛṣṇibhṛtyaiḥ saha prabhuḥ.
english: Then, the mighty Arjuna (Pārtha), also known as Dhananjaya, agitated, struck down the Dasyus with arrows released from his Gāṇḍīva bow, accompanied by the Vrishni soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 58. Then, the mighty Arjuna (Pārtha), also known as Dhananjaya, agitated, struck down the Dasyus with arrows released from his Gāṇḍīva bow, accompanied by the Vrishni soldiers.

sanskrit: क्षणेन तस्य ते राजन्क्षयं जग्मुरजिह्मगाः ।अक्षया हि पुरा भूत्वा क्षीणाः क्षतजभोजनाः ॥५९॥
transliteration: kṣaṇena tasya te rājankṣayaṁ jagmurajihmagāḥ ,akṣayā hi purā bhūtvā kṣīṇāḥ kṣatajabhojanāḥ.
english: O king, his (Arjuna's) straight-flying arrows instantly met their destruction. For, having been inexhaustible before, they were now exhausted, having consumed blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 59. O king, his (Arjuna's) straight-flying arrows instantly met their destruction. For, having been inexhaustible before, they were now exhausted, having consumed blood.

sanskrit: स शरक्षयमासाद्य दुःखशोकसमाहतः ।धनुष्कोट्या तदा दस्यूनवधीत्पाकशासनिः ॥६०॥
transliteration: sa śarakṣayamāsādya duḥkhaśokasamāhataḥ ,dhanuṣkoṭyā tadā dasyūnavadhītpākaśāsaniḥ.
english: Having encountered the exhaustion of his arrows and being overcome with distress and grief, Arjuna, the son of Indra (Pākaśāsani), then killed the bandits (dasyu) with the tip of his bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 60. Having encountered the exhaustion of his arrows and being overcome with distress and grief, Arjuna, the son of Indra (Pākaśāsani), then killed the bandits (dasyu) with the tip of his bow.

sanskrit: प्रेक्षतस्त्वेव पार्थस्य वृष्ण्यन्धकवरस्त्रियः ।जग्मुरादाय ते म्लेच्छाः समन्ताज्जनमेजय ॥६१॥
transliteration: prekṣatastveva pārthasya vṛṣṇyandhakavarastriyaḥ ,jagmurādāya te mlecchāḥ samantājjanamejaya.
english: But, O Janamejaya, while Pārtha (Arjuna) merely watched, those barbarians (mleccha) from all sides seized and carried away the most excellent women of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 61. But, O Janamejaya, while Pārtha (Arjuna) merely watched, those barbarians (mleccha) from all sides seized and carried away the most excellent women of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.

sanskrit: धनंजयस्तु दैवं तन्मनसाचिन्तयत्प्रभुः ।दुःखशोकसमाविष्टो निःश्वासपरमोऽभवत् ॥६२॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayastu daivaṁ tanmanasācintayatprabhuḥ ,duḥkhaśokasamāviṣṭo niḥśvāsaparamo'bhavat.
english: But the mighty Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) pondered that divine will (fate) in his mind. Overwhelmed by distress and grief, he was filled with sighs.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 62. But the mighty Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) pondered that divine will (fate) in his mind. Overwhelmed by distress and grief, he was filled with sighs.

sanskrit: अस्त्राणां च प्रणाशेन बाहुवीर्यस्य संक्षयात् ।धनुषश्चाविधेयत्वाच्छराणां संक्षयेण च ॥६३॥
transliteration: astrāṇāṁ ca praṇāśena bāhuvīryasya saṁkṣayāt ,dhanuṣaścāvidheyatvāccharāṇāṁ saṁkṣayeṇa ca.
english: Due to the destruction of his weapons, the loss of his arm-strength, the unresponsiveness of his bow, and the depletion of his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 63. Due to the destruction of his weapons, the loss of his arm-strength, the unresponsiveness of his bow, and the depletion of his arrows.

sanskrit: बभूव विमनाः पार्थो दैवमित्यनुचिन्तयन् ।न्यवर्तत ततो राजन्नेदमस्तीति चाब्रवीत् ॥६४॥
transliteration: babhūva vimanāḥ pārtho daivamityanucintayan ,nyavartata tato rājannedamastīti cābravīt.
english: Partha (Arjuna) became disheartened, reflecting thus on destiny (daiva). Then, O King, he turned back and said, 'This is no longer possible.'
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 64. Partha (Arjuna) became disheartened, reflecting thus on destiny (daiva). Then, O King, he turned back and said, 'This is no longer possible.'

sanskrit: ततः स शेषमादाय कलत्रस्य महामतिः ।हृतभूयिष्ठरत्नस्य कुरुक्षेत्रमवातरत् ॥६५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa śeṣamādāya kalatrasya mahāmatiḥ ,hṛtabhūyiṣṭharatnasya kurukṣetramavātarat.
english: Then the great-minded (Arjuna), having taken the remainder of the women whose most valuable possessions had been plundered, arrived at Kurukshetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 65. Then the great-minded (Arjuna), having taken the remainder of the women whose most valuable possessions had been plundered, arrived at Kurukshetra.

sanskrit: एवं कलत्रमानीय वृष्णीनां हृतशेषितम् ।न्यवेशयत कौरव्यस्तत्र तत्र धनंजयः ॥६६॥
transliteration: evaṁ kalatramānīya vṛṣṇīnāṁ hṛtaśeṣitam ,nyaveśayata kauravyastatra tatra dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Thus, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the descendant of Kuru (Kauravya), having brought the remaining women of the Vrishnis who had been plundered, settled them in various places.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 66. Thus, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the descendant of Kuru (Kauravya), having brought the remaining women of the Vrishnis who had been plundered, settled them in various places.

sanskrit: हार्दिक्यतनयं पार्थो नगरं मार्तिकावतम् ।भोजराजकलत्रं च हृतशेषं नरोत्तमः ॥६७॥
transliteration: hārdikyatanayaṁ pārtho nagaraṁ mārtikāvatam ,bhojarājakalatraṁ ca hṛtaśeṣaṁ narottamaḥ.
english: The best of men, Pṛthā's son (Arjuna), took Hārdikya's son, the city of Mārtikāvata, and the remaining wives of the Bhoja king after they had been seized.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 67. The best of men, Pṛthā's son (Arjuna), took Hārdikya's son, the city of Mārtikāvata, and the remaining wives of the Bhoja king after they had been seized.

sanskrit: ततो वृद्धांश्च बालांश्च स्त्रियश्चादाय पाण्डवः ।वीरैर्विहीनान्सर्वांस्ताञ्शक्रप्रस्थे न्यवेशयत् ॥६८॥
transliteration: tato vṛddhāṁśca bālāṁśca striyaścādāya pāṇḍavaḥ ,vīrairvihīnānsarvāṁstāñśakraprasthe nyaveśayat.
english: Then the Pāṇḍava, having taken the old people, the children, and the women – all of them devoid of warriors – settled them in Śakraprastha.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 68. Then the Pāṇḍava, having taken the old people, the children, and the women – all of them devoid of warriors – settled them in Śakraprastha.

sanskrit: यौयुधानिं सरस्वत्यां पुत्रं सात्यकिनः प्रियम् ।न्यवेशयत धर्मात्मा वृद्धबालपुरस्कृतम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: yauyudhāniṁ sarasvatyāṁ putraṁ sātyakinaḥ priyam ,nyaveśayata dharmātmā vṛddhabālapuraskṛtam.
english: The one with a righteous soul (dharmātmā) (Arjuna) settled Yuyudhāni, the dear son of Sātyaki, in Sarasvatī, with the old and children at the forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 69. The one with a righteous soul (dharmātmā) (Arjuna) settled Yuyudhāni, the dear son of Sātyaki, in Sarasvatī, with the old and children at the forefront.

sanskrit: इन्द्रप्रस्थे ददौ राज्यं वज्राय परवीरहा ।वज्रेणाक्रूरदारास्तु वार्यमाणाः प्रवव्रजुः ॥७०॥
transliteration: indraprasthe dadau rājyaṁ vajrāya paravīrahā ,vajreṇākrūradārāstu vāryamāṇāḥ pravavrajuḥ.
english: The slayer of enemy heroes (Arjuna) gave the kingdom to Vajra in Indraprastha. However, Akrūra's wives, though being restrained by Vajra, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 70. The slayer of enemy heroes (Arjuna) gave the kingdom to Vajra in Indraprastha. However, Akrūra's wives, though being restrained by Vajra, departed.

sanskrit: रुक्मिणी त्वथ गान्धारी शैब्या हैमवतीत्यपि ।देवी जाम्बवती चैव विविशुर्जातवेदसम् ॥७१॥
transliteration: rukmiṇī tvatha gāndhārī śaibyā haimavatītyapi ,devī jāmbavatī caiva viviśurjātavedasam.
english: Rukmiṇī, then Gāndhārī, Śaibyā, and Haimavatī, as well as the goddess Jāmbavatī, all entered the fire (jātavedasam).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 71. Rukmiṇī, then Gāndhārī, Śaibyā, and Haimavatī, as well as the goddess Jāmbavatī, all entered the fire (jātavedasam).

sanskrit: सत्यभामा तथैवान्या देव्यः कृष्णस्य संमताः ।वनं प्रविविशू राजंस्तापस्ये कृतनिश्चयाः ॥७२॥
transliteration: satyabhāmā tathaivānyā devyaḥ kṛṣṇasya saṁmatāḥ ,vanaṁ praviviśū rājaṁstāpasye kṛtaniścayāḥ.
english: Satyabhāmā, as well as other beloved (saṃmatāḥ) queens of Krishna, O king (rājan), entered the forest, having resolved upon asceticism (tāpasya).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 72. Satyabhāmā, as well as other beloved (saṃmatāḥ) queens of Krishna, O king (rājan), entered the forest, having resolved upon asceticism (tāpasya).

sanskrit: द्वारकावासिनो ये तु पुरुषाः पार्थमन्वयुः ।यथार्हं संविभज्यैनान्वज्रे पर्यददज्जयः ॥७३॥
transliteration: dvārakāvāsino ye tu puruṣāḥ pārthamanvayuḥ ,yathārhaṁ saṁvibhajyainānvajre paryadadajjayaḥ.
english: Those residents of Dvārakā who followed Pārtha (Arjuna) - having duly (yathārham) distributed them, Jaya then entrusted them to Vajra.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 73. Those residents of Dvārakā who followed Pārtha (Arjuna) - having duly (yathārham) distributed them, Jaya then entrusted them to Vajra.

sanskrit: स तत्कृत्वा प्राप्तकालं बाष्पेणापिहितोऽर्जुनः ।कृष्णद्वैपायनं राजन्ददर्शासीनमाश्रमे ॥७४॥
transliteration: sa tatkṛtvā prāptakālaṁ bāṣpeṇāpihito'rjunaḥ ,kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaṁ rājandadarśāsīnamāśrame.
english: Having done that which was appropriate (prāptakālam), Arjuna, his eyes covered with tears, O king (rājan), saw Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) seated in his hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 8 Verse 74. Having done that which was appropriate (prāptakālam), Arjuna, his eyes covered with tears, O king (rājan), saw Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) seated in his hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्रविशन्नर्जुनो राजन्नाश्रमं सत्यवादिनः ।ददर्शासीनमेकान्ते मुनिं सत्यवतीसुतम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,praviśannarjuno rājannāśramaṁ satyavādinaḥ ,dadarśāsīnamekānte muniṁ satyavatīsutam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, as Arjuna entered the hermitage of the truth-speaker, he saw the sage, son of Satyavatī, seated in a secluded spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, as Arjuna entered the hermitage of the truth-speaker, he saw the sage, son of Satyavatī, seated in a secluded spot.

sanskrit: स तमासाद्य धर्मज्ञमुपतस्थे महाव्रतम् ।अर्जुनोऽस्मीति नामास्मै निवेद्याभ्यवदत्ततः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa tamāsādya dharmajñamupatasthe mahāvratam ,arjuno'smīti nāmāsmai nivedyābhyavadattataḥ.
english: Having approached that great ascetic, the knower of natural law (dharma), Arjuna announced his name, saying "I am Arjuna," and then spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. Having approached that great ascetic, the knower of natural law (dharma), Arjuna announced his name, saying "I am Arjuna," and then spoke to him.

sanskrit: स्वागतं तेऽस्त्विति प्राह मुनिः सत्यवतीसुतः ।आस्यतामिति चोवाच प्रसन्नात्मा महामुनिः ॥३॥
transliteration: svāgataṁ te'stviti prāha muniḥ satyavatīsutaḥ ,āsyatāmiti covāca prasannātmā mahāmuniḥ.
english: The sage, son of Satyavatī, with a serene mind, said, "Welcome to you," and also, "Please be seated."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. The sage, son of Satyavatī, with a serene mind, said, "Welcome to you," and also, "Please be seated."

sanskrit: तमप्रतीतमनसं निःश्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः ।निर्विण्णमनसं दृष्ट्वा पार्थं व्यासोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tamapratītamanasaṁ niḥśvasantaṁ punaḥ punaḥ ,nirviṇṇamanasaṁ dṛṣṭvā pārthaṁ vyāso'bravīdidam.
english: Seeing Pārtha, distressed and dejected in mind, and sighing repeatedly, Vyāsa spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Seeing Pārtha, distressed and dejected in mind, and sighing repeatedly, Vyāsa spoke these words.

sanskrit: अवीरजोऽभिघातस्ते ब्राह्मणो वा हतस्त्वया ।युद्धे पराजितो वासि गतश्रीरिव लक्ष्यसे ॥५॥
transliteration: avīrajo'bhighātaste brāhmaṇo vā hatastvayā ,yuddhe parājito vāsi gataśrīriva lakṣyase.
english: Has your vigor been diminished, or have you killed a brahmin, or have you been defeated in battle? You appear as if your glory has departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. Has your vigor been diminished, or have you killed a brahmin, or have you been defeated in battle? You appear as if your glory has departed.

sanskrit: न त्वा प्रत्यभिजानामि किमिदं भरतर्षभ ।श्रोतव्यं चेन्मया पार्थ क्षिप्रमाख्यातुमर्हसि ॥६॥
transliteration: na tvā pratyabhijānāmi kimidaṁ bharatarṣabha ,śrotavyaṁ cenmayā pārtha kṣipramākhyātumarhasi.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, I do not recognize you. What is this (state of affairs)? O Pārtha, if it is to be heard by me, you ought to tell it quickly.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. O best of the Bhāratas, I do not recognize you. What is this (state of affairs)? O Pārtha, if it is to be heard by me, you ought to tell it quickly.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।यः स मेघवपुः श्रीमान्बृहत्पङ्कजलोचनः ।स कृष्णः सह रामेण त्यक्त्वा देहं दिवं गतः ॥७॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,yaḥ sa meghavapuḥ śrīmānbṛhatpaṅkajalocanaḥ ,sa kṛṣṇaḥ saha rāmeṇa tyaktvā dehaṁ divaṁ gataḥ.
english: Arjuna said: That glorious one, whose form was like a cloud and whose eyes were like large lotuses - that (kṛṣṇa) has gone to heaven, having given up his body along with (rāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. Arjuna said: That glorious one, whose form was like a cloud and whose eyes were like large lotuses - that (kṛṣṇa) has gone to heaven, having given up his body along with (rāma).

sanskrit: मौसले वृष्णिवीराणां विनाशो ब्रह्मशापजः ।बभूव वीरान्तकरः प्रभासे रोमहर्षणः ॥८॥
transliteration: mausale vṛṣṇivīrāṇāṁ vināśo brahmaśāpajaḥ ,babhūva vīrāntakaraḥ prabhāse romaharṣaṇaḥ.
english: The destruction of the Vṛṣṇi heroes, caused by a brahmin's curse, took place in Prabhāsa during the Mauṣala event, a horrifying and hero-ending occurrence.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. The destruction of the Vṛṣṇi heroes, caused by a brahmin's curse, took place in Prabhāsa during the Mauṣala event, a horrifying and hero-ending occurrence.

sanskrit: ये ते शूरा महात्मानः सिंहदर्पा महाबलाः ।भोजवृष्ण्यन्धका ब्रह्मन्नन्योन्यं तैर्हतं युधि ॥९॥
transliteration: ye te śūrā mahātmānaḥ siṁhadarpā mahābalāḥ ,bhojavṛṣṇyandhakā brahmannanyonyaṁ tairhataṁ yudhi.
english: O brahmin (brahman)! Those valiant, great-souled heroes, mighty as lions, the Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, and Andhakas, were slain by each other in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. O brahmin (brahman)! Those valiant, great-souled heroes, mighty as lions, the Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, and Andhakas, were slain by each other in battle.

sanskrit: गदापरिघशक्तीनां सहाः परिघबाहवः ।त एरकाभिर्निहताः पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: gadāparighaśaktīnāṁ sahāḥ parighabāhavaḥ ,ta erakābhirnihatāḥ paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: Those whose arms were strong like iron maces, capable of bearing maces, iron bars, and spears, were destroyed by blades of grass. Behold the reversal of time (kāla)!
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. Those whose arms were strong like iron maces, capable of bearing maces, iron bars, and spears, were destroyed by blades of grass. Behold the reversal of time (kāla)!

sanskrit: हतं पञ्चशतं तेषां सहस्रं बाहुशालिनाम् ।निधनं समनुप्राप्तं समासाद्येतरेतरम् ॥११॥
transliteration: hataṁ pañcaśataṁ teṣāṁ sahasraṁ bāhuśālinām ,nidhanaṁ samanuprāptaṁ samāsādyetaretaram.
english: Five hundred of them were slain, a thousand of the powerful-armed ones met destruction, having attacked each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. Five hundred of them were slain, a thousand of the powerful-armed ones met destruction, having attacked each other.

sanskrit: पुनः पुनर्न मृष्यामि विनाशममितौजसाम् ।चिन्तयानो यदूनां च कृष्णस्य च यशस्विनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: punaḥ punarna mṛṣyāmi vināśamamitaujasām ,cintayāno yadūnāṁ ca kṛṣṇasya ca yaśasvinaḥ.
english: Again and again, I cannot bear the destruction of those with immeasurable prowess. I meditate on the Yadus and the glorious Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. Again and again, I cannot bear the destruction of those with immeasurable prowess. I meditate on the Yadus and the glorious Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: शोषणं सागरस्येव पर्वतस्येव चालनम् ।नभसः पतनं चैव शैत्यमग्नेस्तथैव च ॥१३॥
transliteration: śoṣaṇaṁ sāgarasyeva parvatasyeva cālanam ,nabhasaḥ patanaṁ caiva śaityamagnestathaiva ca.
english: It is like the drying up of the ocean, the moving of a mountain, the falling of the sky, and similarly, the coldness of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. It is like the drying up of the ocean, the moving of a mountain, the falling of the sky, and similarly, the coldness of fire.

sanskrit: अश्रद्धेयमहं मन्ये विनाशं शार्ङ्गधन्वनः ।न चेह स्थातुमिच्छामि लोके कृष्णविनाकृतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: aśraddheyamahaṁ manye vināśaṁ śārṅgadhanvanaḥ ,na ceha sthātumicchāmi loke kṛṣṇavinākṛtaḥ.
english: I consider the demise of the wielder of the Śārṅga bow to be unbelievable. Nor do I wish to remain in this world, bereft of Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. I consider the demise of the wielder of the Śārṅga bow to be unbelievable. Nor do I wish to remain in this world, bereft of Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: इतः कष्टतरं चान्यच्छृणु तद्वै तपोधन ।मनो मे दीर्यते येन चिन्तयानस्य वै मुहुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: itaḥ kaṣṭataraṁ cānyacchṛṇu tadvai tapodhana ,mano me dīryate yena cintayānasya vai muhuḥ.
english: O ascetic (tapodhana), listen indeed to something else even more grievous than this, by which my mind is repeatedly torn apart as I reflect.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. O ascetic (tapodhana), listen indeed to something else even more grievous than this, by which my mind is repeatedly torn apart as I reflect.

sanskrit: पश्यतो वृष्णिदाराश्च मम ब्रह्मन्सहस्रशः ।आभीरैरनुसृत्याजौ हृताः पञ्चनदालयैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: paśyato vṛṣṇidārāśca mama brahmansahasraśaḥ ,ābhīrairanusṛtyājau hṛtāḥ pañcanadālayaiḥ.
english: O Brahmin, while I watched, thousands of my Vṛṣṇi women were carried away by the Abhīras, the dwellers of Pañcanada, who pursued them in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. O Brahmin, while I watched, thousands of my Vṛṣṇi women were carried away by the Abhīras, the dwellers of Pañcanada, who pursued them in battle.

sanskrit: धनुरादाय तत्राहं नाशकं तस्य पूरणे ।यथा पुरा च मे वीर्यं भुजयोर्न तथाभवत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: dhanurādāya tatrāhaṁ nāśakaṁ tasya pūraṇe ,yathā purā ca me vīryaṁ bhujayorna tathābhavat.
english: When I took up the bow there, I was unable to string it. My strength in my arms was no longer what it used to be.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. When I took up the bow there, I was unable to string it. My strength in my arms was no longer what it used to be.

sanskrit: अस्त्राणि मे प्रनष्टानि विविधानि महामुने ।शराश्च क्षयमापन्नाः क्षणेनैव समन्ततः ॥१८॥
transliteration: astrāṇi me pranaṣṭāni vividhāni mahāmune ,śarāśca kṣayamāpannāḥ kṣaṇenaiva samantataḥ.
english: O great sage (muni), my various weapons were lost, and the arrows were completely exhausted in an instant from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. O great sage (muni), my various weapons were lost, and the arrows were completely exhausted in an instant from all sides.

sanskrit: पुरुषश्चाप्रमेयात्मा शङ्खचक्रगदाधरः ।चतुर्भुजः पीतवासा श्यामः पद्मायतेक्षणः ॥१९॥
transliteration: puruṣaścāprameyātmā śaṅkhacakragadādharaḥ ,caturbhujaḥ pītavāsā śyāmaḥ padmāyatekṣaṇaḥ.
english: And the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa), whose true nature (ātman) is immeasurable, bears the conch, discus, and mace. He is four-armed, dressed in yellow garments, dark-complexioned, and has eyes like elongated lotus petals.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. And the supreme cosmic person (puruṣa), whose true nature (ātman) is immeasurable, bears the conch, discus, and mace. He is four-armed, dressed in yellow garments, dark-complexioned, and has eyes like elongated lotus petals.

sanskrit: यः स याति पुरस्तान्मे रथस्य सुमहाद्युतिः ।प्रदहन्रिपुसैन्यानि न पश्याम्यहमद्य तम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yaḥ sa yāti purastānme rathasya sumahādyutiḥ ,pradahanripusainyāni na paśyāmyahamadya tam.
english: I do not see him today, the one of immense splendor who used to go before my chariot, incinerating enemy armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. I do not see him today, the one of immense splendor who used to go before my chariot, incinerating enemy armies.

sanskrit: येन पूर्वं प्रदग्धानि शत्रुसैन्यानि तेजसा ।शरैर्गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैरहं पश्चाद्व्यनाशयम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: yena pūrvaṁ pradagdhāni śatrusainyāni tejasā ,śarairgāṇḍīvanirmuktairahaṁ paścādvyanāśayam.
english: By whose (Krishna's) prowess the enemy armies were previously incinerated, I subsequently annihilated them with arrows shot from the Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. By whose (Krishna's) prowess the enemy armies were previously incinerated, I subsequently annihilated them with arrows shot from the Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: तमपश्यन्विषीदामि घूर्णामीव च सत्तम ।परिनिर्विण्णचेताश्च शान्तिं नोपलभेऽपि च ॥२२॥
transliteration: tamapaśyanviṣīdāmi ghūrṇāmīva ca sattama ,parinirviṇṇacetāśca śāntiṁ nopalabhe'pi ca.
english: Not seeing him (Krishna), O best among the virtuous, I grieve and feel as though I am reeling. With a completely dejected mind, I also find no peace (śānti).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. Not seeing him (Krishna), O best among the virtuous, I grieve and feel as though I am reeling. With a completely dejected mind, I also find no peace (śānti).

sanskrit: विना जनार्दनं वीरं नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे ।श्रुत्वैव हि गतं विष्णुं ममापि मुमुहुर्दिशः ॥२३॥
transliteration: vinā janārdanaṁ vīraṁ nāhaṁ jīvitumutsahe ,śrutvaiva hi gataṁ viṣṇuṁ mamāpi mumuhurdiśaḥ.
english: Without the heroic Janārdana, I am unable to live. For, merely hearing that Viṣṇu (viṣṇu) has departed, even the directions became bewildered for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. Without the heroic Janārdana, I am unable to live. For, merely hearing that Viṣṇu (viṣṇu) has departed, even the directions became bewildered for me.

sanskrit: प्रनष्टज्ञातिवीर्यस्य शून्यस्य परिधावतः ।उपदेष्टुं मम श्रेयो भवानर्हति सत्तम ॥२४॥
transliteration: pranaṣṭajñātivīryasya śūnyasya paridhāvataḥ ,upadeṣṭuṁ mama śreyo bhavānarhati sattama.
english: For me, who has lost all relatives and valor, who feels empty and wanders aimlessly, you, O best among the virtuous, should instruct me on what is truly beneficial.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. For me, who has lost all relatives and valor, who feels empty and wanders aimlessly, you, O best among the virtuous, should instruct me on what is truly beneficial.

sanskrit: व्यास उवाच ।ब्रह्मशापविनिर्दग्धा वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथाः ।विनष्टाः कुरुशार्दूल न ताञ्शोचितुमर्हसि ॥२५॥
transliteration: vyāsa uvāca ,brahmaśāpavinirdagdhā vṛṣṇyandhakamahārathāḥ ,vinaṣṭāḥ kuruśārdūla na tāñśocitumarhasi.
english: Vyāsa said: "O tiger among the Kurus, the great charioteers of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas have perished, burnt by a brahmin's curse. You should not grieve for them."
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. Vyāsa said: "O tiger among the Kurus, the great charioteers of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas have perished, burnt by a brahmin's curse. You should not grieve for them."

sanskrit: भवितव्यं तथा तद्धि दिष्टमेतन्महात्मनाम् ।उपेक्षितं च कृष्णेन शक्तेनापि व्यपोहितुम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhavitavyaṁ tathā taddhi diṣṭametanmahātmanām ,upekṣitaṁ ca kṛṣṇena śaktenāpi vyapohitum.
english: Indeed, that was destined to happen; it was fate for these great souls (mahātman). Kṛṣṇa, though capable of averting it, chose to overlook it.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 26. Indeed, that was destined to happen; it was fate for these great souls (mahātman). Kṛṣṇa, though capable of averting it, chose to overlook it.

sanskrit: त्रैलोक्यमपि कृष्णो हि कृत्स्नं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ।प्रसहेदन्यथा कर्तुं किमु शापं मनीषिणाम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: trailokyamapi kṛṣṇo hi kṛtsnaṁ sthāvarajaṅgamam ,prasahedanyathā kartuṁ kimu śāpaṁ manīṣiṇām.
english: Indeed, Kṛṣṇa could have transformed the entire three worlds, including all movable and immovable beings. What then to say of the curse of the wise ones?
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 27. Indeed, Kṛṣṇa could have transformed the entire three worlds, including all movable and immovable beings. What then to say of the curse of the wise ones?

sanskrit: रथस्य पुरतो याति यः स चक्रगदाधरः ।तव स्नेहात्पुराणर्षिर्वासुदेवश्चतुर्भुजः ॥२८॥
transliteration: rathasya purato yāti yaḥ sa cakragadādharaḥ ,tava snehātpurāṇarṣirvāsudevaścaturbhujaḥ.
english: He who goes in front of your chariot, out of affection for you, is that Vāsudeva, the wielder of the discus and mace, the ancient sage, the four-armed one.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 28. He who goes in front of your chariot, out of affection for you, is that Vāsudeva, the wielder of the discus and mace, the ancient sage, the four-armed one.

sanskrit: कृत्वा भारावतरणं पृथिव्याः पृथुलोचनः ।मोक्षयित्वा जगत्सर्वं गतः स्वस्थानमुत्तमम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: kṛtvā bhārāvataraṇaṁ pṛthivyāḥ pṛthulocanaḥ ,mokṣayitvā jagatsarvaṁ gataḥ svasthānamuttamam.
english: The wide-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa), having relieved the earth of its burden and liberated the entire world, returned to his supreme abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 29. The wide-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa), having relieved the earth of its burden and liberated the entire world, returned to his supreme abode.

sanskrit: त्वया त्विह महत्कर्म देवानां पुरुषर्षभ ।कृतं भीमसहायेन यमाभ्यां च महाभुज ॥३०॥
transliteration: tvayā tviha mahatkarma devānāṁ puruṣarṣabha ,kṛtaṁ bhīmasahāyena yamābhyāṁ ca mahābhuja.
english: O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), O mighty-armed one (mahābhuja), a great task (karma) for the gods has indeed been accomplished by you here, with the assistance of Bhīma and the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 30. O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), O mighty-armed one (mahābhuja), a great task (karma) for the gods has indeed been accomplished by you here, with the assistance of Bhīma and the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva).

sanskrit: कृतकृत्यांश्च वो मन्ये संसिद्धान्कुरुपुंगव ।गमनं प्राप्तकालं च तद्धि श्रेयो मतं मम ॥३१॥
transliteration: kṛtakṛtyāṁśca vo manye saṁsiddhānkurupuṁgava ,gamanaṁ prāptakālaṁ ca taddhi śreyo mataṁ mama.
english: O best of the Kurus (kurupuṃgava), I consider you to have accomplished your purpose and to be perfected. Indeed, a timely departure is deemed by me to be the most beneficial.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 31. O best of the Kurus (kurupuṃgava), I consider you to have accomplished your purpose and to be perfected. Indeed, a timely departure is deemed by me to be the most beneficial.

sanskrit: बलं बुद्धिश्च तेजश्च प्रतिपत्तिश्च भारत ।भवन्ति भवकालेषु विपद्यन्ते विपर्यये ॥३२॥
transliteration: balaṁ buddhiśca tejaśca pratipattiśca bhārata ,bhavanti bhavakāleṣu vipadyante viparyaye.
english: O Bhārata, strength, intelligence, vitality, and discernment flourish in prosperous times, but they diminish in adversity.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 32. O Bhārata, strength, intelligence, vitality, and discernment flourish in prosperous times, but they diminish in adversity.

sanskrit: कालमूलमिदं सर्वं जगद्बीजं धनंजय ।काल एव समादत्ते पुनरेव यदृच्छया ॥३३॥
transliteration: kālamūlamidaṁ sarvaṁ jagadbījaṁ dhanaṁjaya ,kāla eva samādatte punareva yadṛcchayā.
english: O Dhananjaya, this entire creation, whose origin and seed is time, time itself reabsorbs again according to its own will.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 33. O Dhananjaya, this entire creation, whose origin and seed is time, time itself reabsorbs again according to its own will.

sanskrit: स एव बलवान्भूत्वा पुनर्भवति दुर्बलः ।स एवेशश्च भूत्वेह परैराज्ञाप्यते पुनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa eva balavānbhūtvā punarbhavati durbalaḥ ,sa eveśaśca bhūtveha parairājñāpyate punaḥ.
english: He alone, having become strong, again becomes weak. He alone, having become a lord here, is again commanded by others.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 34. He alone, having become strong, again becomes weak. He alone, having become a lord here, is again commanded by others.

sanskrit: कृतकृत्यानि चास्त्राणि गतान्यद्य यथागतम् ।पुनरेष्यन्ति ते हस्तं यदा कालो भविष्यति ॥३५॥
transliteration: kṛtakṛtyāni cāstrāṇi gatānyadya yathāgatam ,punareṣyanti te hastaṁ yadā kālo bhaviṣyati.
english: And the weapons, having fulfilled their purpose, have now departed as they came. They will return to your hand again when the time comes.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 35. And the weapons, having fulfilled their purpose, have now departed as they came. They will return to your hand again when the time comes.

sanskrit: कालो गन्तुं गतिं मुख्यां भवतामपि भारत ।एतच्छ्रेयो हि वो मन्ये परमं भरतर्षभ ॥३६॥
transliteration: kālo gantuṁ gatiṁ mukhyāṁ bhavatāmapi bhārata ,etacchreyo hi vo manye paramaṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O Bharata, it is time for you all to proceed towards the highest state. Indeed, O best of the Bharatas, I consider this to be the supreme welfare for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 36. O Bharata, it is time for you all to proceed towards the highest state. Indeed, O best of the Bharatas, I consider this to be the supreme welfare for you.

sanskrit: एतद्वचनमाज्ञाय व्यासस्यामिततेजसः ।अनुज्ञातो ययौ पार्थो नगरं नागसाह्वयम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: etadvacanamājñāya vyāsasyāmitatejasaḥ ,anujñāto yayau pārtho nagaraṁ nāgasāhvayam.
english: Having received this instruction from Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable spiritual power, Arjuna (Pārtha) then proceeded to the city known as Nāgasāhvaya (Hastinapura) after obtaining his permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 37. Having received this instruction from Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable spiritual power, Arjuna (Pārtha) then proceeded to the city known as Nāgasāhvaya (Hastinapura) after obtaining his permission.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य च पुरीं वीरः समासाद्य युधिष्ठिरम् ।आचष्ट तद्यथावृत्तं वृष्ण्यन्धकजनं प्रति ॥३८॥
transliteration: praviśya ca purīṁ vīraḥ samāsādya yudhiṣṭhiram ,ācaṣṭa tadyathāvṛttaṁ vṛṣṇyandhakajanaṁ prati.
english: And having entered the city, the hero (Arjuna), after meeting Yudhiṣṭhira, narrated all that had transpired concerning the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans.
search_text: Mahabharata Mausala Parva Chapter 9 Verse 38. And having entered the city, the hero (Arjuna), after meeting Yudhiṣṭhira, narrated all that had transpired concerning the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans.
